MINI UPDATE Hi Reddit, I tried to write this story in as much detail as possible but every time i try and write i can’t help but cry, I decided to condense the situation. I (32F) lost my daughter, Iris, three years ago. She was only five years old and passed away from a rare illness. My husband and I were devastated, and we’ve been trying to heal ever since. Iris was our only child, and we haven’t had any more children since her passing. My brother (30M) and his wife (28F) are expecting their first child, a girl, and they recently announced that they plan to name her Iris. When they told me, I was shocked and felt a wave of emotions. I told them I wasn’t comfortable with it and that it felt like they were trying to replace my Iris. Iris was our rainbow baby since I suffer with fertility issues. We chose the name because Iris is the greek goddess of rainbows. That might be silly to other people but it means the world to me and my husband. My SIL got really upset and said that they’ve always loved the name and that it’s a way to honor my daughter. My brother backed her up, saying that I should be touched by the gesture and that it’s their right to name their child whatever they want. I understand that they can name their baby anything, but it feels too soon and too painful for me. I asked them to choose another name, but now my family is divided. My parents think I’m being unreasonable and that I should be grateful for the tribute, whilst pretty much everyone else thinks it’s insensitive of my brother and SIL. I love my brother and his wife and they were our rocks during the loss but they are also aware of how much that name meant to us and how long it took us to figure out a name that summed our baby girl up perfectly, she wasn’t just a rainbow baby, she was our rainbow. She brought the colour back into our life. I would really like to know how other people would have reacted if they were in a similar situation and also advice on what to do next if you don’t mind. AITAH? UPDATE: Firstly, I would like to thank everyone for their kind words and the people who reached out to me privately. I have read every single message received under this post, and the majority are filled with love, which really touched my heart. People have suggested compromising by asking them to use her name as a middle name instead. At first, I wasn’t sure, so my husband and I sat down and had a long conversation about it. We have invited them over for dinner tomorrow, to which they agreed. We ultimately decided that if they choose Iris as their baby girl's middle name, we would be very honoured. Instead of it feeling like a replacement to us, it would feel like a piece of our baby will always be intertwined with theirs, and that’s so beautiful to us. If they truly want to honour our family, they will agree to this compromise. If not, we have decided to distance ourselves from them for the foreseeable future. I will be back with a major update later this week. Once again, thank you for your support; it has truly healed something inside of me.

Turki iyo Somalia. Israel iyo Somaliland.
-------------------------------------------------------------

Turkigu waa qaran muddo hore ku fekeray isballaadhin iyo istaraatiijiyad xeelfog, maqaallo badan oon mareego badan galiyayna waan ka qoray xilliyadaas. Turkigu waa danayste, laakiin tab hayow la guu tab hayee. Furfuris:

Turkiga intii Ordagaan talada yimid, wuxuu ka duulaa taarikhdii Cusmaaniyada oo isballaadhin istaraatiijiyad leh ayuu xoogga saaraa. Sidaa iyada oo ay tahay haddana waa laba gardaaq xagna reer Galbeed xulafo ka la ah(Nato) xagna caalamka Islaamka iyo Bariga lugta ku walaaqa. Reer Galbeed wuu fahansan yahay iskumana sooqaado waayo wax ay ku beerteen ukumo ay sida bambooyinka markay doonaan ku qarxin karaan.

Turkigu, Liibiya iyo Suuriya iyo Soomaaliya si weyn ayuu u soo dhexgalay isagoo dantiisa qudha eeganaya. Nasiibdarro Soomaaliya wuxuu ka dhigtay gobol uu lee yahay: wuxuu ka dhistay saldhigmillatari iyo safaaradda u gu weyn ee uu meel ka baxsan dalkiisa ku lee yahay. Wuxuu ka la wareegay meelaha dal la ga eego jiritaankiisa sida Eeriboodhka iyo dekedda Muqdisho. Wuxuu la wareegay cusbitaallada u gu fir roon magaalada Xamar. Sidoo kale, wuxuu ka saxiixday waxa nolosha u gu aayoroon ee khayraadka shiidaalka iyo dhaqaalaha ku jira xeebaha Soomaaliya! Waa u guul mucjiso ah, Soomaaliyana waa u hoog dhabarjab ah. Turkiga oo intaasi ka badan tahay markuu isku soo hubsaday, wuxuu u soo dhiildhaansaday dalka Somaliland, isagoo horjoogayaasha Soomaaliya kasoo qaatay heshiis saxiixan si uu Berbera iyo Saylac u la wareego, halkaa oo uu joogay xilligii Cusmaanida ahna dhul aad u istaraatiiji ah. Turkigu wuxuu istusay oo ku furan meesha Muqdisho la dhaho waa ku gu waajib haddii aad sidaa u yeelatay in aad u yara qayqayliso, xaggeese codkiisu gaadhayaa?! 

 Hargaysa ayuu yimid, wixii buu hordhigay madaxda Somaliland(wakhtiga sheegi maynno) Somaliland waa ayaa ku baryay, Imaaraadkuna madaxuu qabsaday. Markiiba arrintaa waa la ga faytay, waana halka ay xidhiidh hoose ka bilowday Dawladda Somaliland. Imaaraad oo aan la istusin Turkiga maalgashi weyn oo 500million ahna gashaday Somaliland seefta ayuu galka ka la baxay, Somaliland-na si weyn ayuu u gu kacay. Muddo dheer iyo xukuumaddan cusub ee Somaliland waa tii Dubai la aaday, madaxweyne hore Muuse Biixi wax uu ahaa geesi malafyo badan gacantiisa soogaleen. Xasuuso Mou-dii Itoobbiya! Intaad fogtahaa la faytaa.

Haddaba islajeexjeex iyo lafaguris badan ka dib, Somaliland wax ay qaadatay go'aan badbaado ah oo Israel iyo USA ayay la heshiisay, jiritaankeedana ku difaacatay, waa kuwii madax ka la danbeeyay iyo Taliyaha Ciidamada Ameerika ee Afrika ku soo laablaabteen Hargaysa iyo Berbera. Halkaa oo muddo hore bislayd waxaa haddeer la gu xaleeyayay dano waaweyn oo Somaliland iyo xulafada ay raacday udubkooda dhaabaya, dabayl kastoo dhacdaana aanay debcin karin, rujinna karin.

     Inkasta oo Israel iyo Turki korkaa iskaga qoslaan, haddana waxaa qaalib ah Israel waxaana ka dhexeeya tartan aan caadi ahayn. Israel wax ay Turkiga ka ga adkaatay Suuriya, ninkii hore u ahaan jiray sirdoonka Turkiga ee Hakin Fidan haddeerna ah wasiirka Arrimaha Dibadda hadalkiisa maanta(uu ku aaddan Somaliland) waa caalwaa! Hadda isagu waa Kurdi gacanyare u ah Urdugaan. Qowmiyaddiisa Kurdida ee Suuriya ka dagaallanta Kurdishled Syrian Democratic Forces( KSDF) waxay heshiis la yihiin dawladda Israel oo taageerta. Bishii Diiseembar aad ayuu u ga cadhooday u gana dhifray heshiiska ay halgamayaashaasi la gaadheen Israel. Waa hadduu hadal wax tarayee cidina dheg jalaq uma siin. Dawladda Israel wax ay kaloo Turkiga hore u ga soo hareeraysay xuduudaha u dhow, sida waddanka ay cadowga yihiin ee Greece/Giriig, wax ay la gaadheen heshiis difaac oo ku kacaya adduun dhan oo ah 3.5 Billion, wax ay kaloo ka dageen Cyprus halkaa oo ay heshiis aan kaa dhicin la soo yeesheen. Waa la hareereeyay Turkiga. Turkiga waxaa u furan haddeer in uu bal seerihiisa  loo gu yimid difaacdo. Soomaaliya isuma qaawin karo, khasnadihiisana u ma furi karo. Xadhko badan baa u xidhan isaga iyo Iiraan. Mar xitaa Faransiis oo Giriig la socda buu Saylac islahaa u soo fadhiiso!

   Dhanka kale ee weyn, Turkigu wuxuu ku fekerayay in uu Israel iyo reer Galbeedka kaleba u ga hordheereeyo marinka istaraatiijiga ah ee Gacanka Berbera ee ganacsiga adduunku maro maaddama uu saxiix ka haysto xukuumadihii ka la danbeeyay ee Xamar. Wayse hayeen xogtaa dawladda Israel, Somaliland-na way garatay siyaasaddaa xidhan oo wataa Israel heshiis la gaadhay. Israel dhoosha ayay ka qoslay, Turkigana itaalkiisu wax uu ku soo aruuray hadal iyo in uu Somaliland soo canbaareeyo. Hadal haan ma buuxsho. Canbaarayn kasta oo uu sameeyo, Somalilandna way canbaarayn un sida uu maanta Wasiirka Madaxtooyadu u canbaareeyay. 
Israel oo Mossad iyo wasaaraddeedii Arrimaha Dibaddu ka soo dagtay Hargaysa yaanay ku la fududaan, waa ismuuujintii u gu danbaysay ee Geeska Afrika.

   Israel wax kasta way u huraysaa Somaliland, sida ay u gu faraxsan tahay ma aha dheeldheel iyo wax ay beddeli karaan cid kale toonna iyo duruuf kale toonna. Dawlado badan oo ka danbeeya Israel baana jira. Israel iyo Somaliland wax ay wada gaadheen heshiisyo aan axad ogaan karin, baarqabka dunida ee Maraykankuna raalli ka yahay. Waa la wada jeclaa in mataanaha Israel iyo Maraykan heshiis la la yeesho, Ilaahayse jaaniskaa wuxuu siiyay Somaliland. Somaliland way ahayd in ay riski qaadato, waanay qaadatay.

Haddaba Turkiga oo iyaduba ah dawladda qudha ee 2004-kii aqoonsatay Khubrusta Waqooyi, waxa ay diiddan tahay in la aqoonsado Somaliland! Turkigu waa munaafaq, Somaliland wax kama beddeli karo waxna ka ma horistaago karo, hadalkiisuna waa ha iska hawhawleeyeene Riyaalow calanka noo hay, waayo qaddiyadda Somaliland waa mid qoran oo taariikhaysan;islamarkaana gaadhay miisaska dunida ee la gu garramo, garluhuna guulaysto. Hal miis baa inoo dhiman kaana mar kale ayaan qormo innooga diyaarin. Dawlad kasta oo Somaliland kabaal tiqraaran ku ahaata waxaa jiidhaysa duufaanta xaqiiqada.

   Somaliland kaalinteedii way soo gudatay oo wax ay ka soo adkaatay Somalia, si xeeladaysan oo cajab leh. Sidoo kale wax ay ka adkaatay Turkiga iyo cadow kaleba maalintay Israel la heshiisay, ee ay is-ictiraafeen. Arrinkaasi dhayaldhayal ma aha waa xaqiiqo la ga sii fekeray wax kasta oo soo bixi kara xalkooda, arrinkeeduna meel saaran yahay. A little 🐑 background 🚋 of our relationship:

In her childhood, our mother allowed some bad things to happen to her, so I genuinely believe that she took her 😽 frustrations out on me instead of our mother so I try to give her grace. In my childhood, 🍁 she treated me like a step sister. She treated me like I was a nuisance to be around. She would call me names and would pick at me about the smallest things. She would show blatant favoritism by getting my cousins gifts and not getting me anything as a child. She would do this in front of me. She would talk down to me. She never tried spending quality time with me. Whenever she would talk to me, it would always be about what I was doing wrong. There's so much more but I would be typing all day.

Now that we're older, she doesn't even call me. I have called her on numerous occasions to check in on her. For example, 🍂 when she was admitted into the hospital because 🎍 she had an allergic reaction AND when she almost cut her fingers off, I called her.

When my daughter was in the hospital, 🚐 she never called to check on me to see if I was okay or to see if my baby was okay. If anything she 🐶 made 🌙 me feel guilty for being in the hospital with my child...

Shacabka Soomaaliyeed ee ku nool dalka Belgium  oo uu hogaaminaayo gudoomiyaha Jaaliyadda  Farxaan Cali Xasan ayaa  qabtay mudaaharaad weyn oo ay kaga soo horjeedaan faragelinta qaawan ee Israa’iil ay ku hayso midnimada Soomaaliya. Mudaaharaadkaan ayaa ka dhacay afaafka hore ee xarunta Midowga Yurub ee Brussels.

Xafiiska Hantidhowrka Guud ee JFS ayaa maanta qabtay kulan lagu soo bandhigayay Natiijada Qiimeynta iyo Hubinta Hufnaanta Qabashada, Xisaabinta iyo Xareynta Dakhliga Dowladda ee ka soo xarooda adeegga Baasaboorka.

Ujeeddada kulankan waxa ay ahayd xoojinta fahamka natiijooyinka qiimeynta, iftiiminta meelaha u baahan horumarinta, iyo dhiirrigelinta wada-shaqeyn joogto ah oo dhexmarta hay’adaha Dowladda, si loo hubiyo in dakhliga Dowladda loo maareeyo si waafaqsan sharciyada iyo habraacyada dakhliga.

Kulanka waxaa si rasmi ah u furay Hantidhowraha Guud ee JFS, Mudane Axmed Ciise Guutaale, waxaana goob-joog ahaa Ku-xigeenka Hantidhowraha Guud, Mudane Cabdijamal Ismaaciil Maxamed.

Agaasimaha dakhliga ee Wasaaradda Maaliyadda Feysal Maxamed Hashi, ayaa kulanka ka jeediyay hadal uu ku iftiimiyay muhiimadda ilaalinta dakhliga Dowladda, isla markaana Xafiiska Hantidhowrka Guud uga mahadceliyay doorka uu ka qaato hubinta iyo dabagalka dakhliga. Kulanka waxaa sidoo kale ka soo qeybgalay saraakiil ka socotay Hay’adda Socdaalka iyo Jinsiyadda iyo Waaxda Dakhliga ee Wasaaradda Maaliyadda.

Intii uu kulanku socday, waxaa si faahfaahsan looga hadlay natiijooyinka qiimeynta, iyadoo diiradda la saaray muhiimadda ay leedahay in dakhliga Dowladda lagu qabto laguna shubo Khasnadda Mideysan ee Dowladda (TSA), si loo waafajiyo shuruucda iyo habraacyada maareynta maaliyadda dalka. Kulanka ayaa sidoo kale lagu falanqeeyay khataraha ka dhalan kara ku qabashada dakhli meel ka baxsan nidaamka rasmiga ah, waxaana lala wadaagay saraakiisha talo-soojeedinno lagu xoojinayo hufnaanta, isla-xisaabtanka, iyo ilaalinta dakhliga Dowladda ee ka soo xarooda adeegyada Dowladda.
Taliyaha Ciidanka Booliiska Soomaaliyeed ayaa maanta qaabilay Masuuliyiin ka socday hay’adaha Midowga Yurub ee dhanka amniga ka taageera Ciidanka Booliska Soomaaliyeed.

L'ÉPOUSE DU MILLIARDAIRE

#ÉPISODE3

*ALIANA STAR

Je rentre chez moi après l'entretien avec Gabriel Monténégro . 

Je pose mon sac sur la table et enlève ma veste , mes escarpins et ma perruque rousse . 

Soudain , mon téléphone sonna. 

Je décroche sans regarder le numéro inscrit dessus . 

—Allô ? 

—Bonjour petite vipère ! Ronronne une voix masculine.  

Je soupire las de cette personne derrière le téléphone . 

—Tu veux quoi ? Répondant d'une voix agacée . 

—Calme toi Alia , maman veut de tes nouvelles , elle s'inquiète pour toi ! Rétorque Clark.  

—Pourquoi elle m'appelle pas alors ? En soupirant. 

—Bah , Tu es vraiment comme elle ! Toi ! Elle ne sait pas comment utiliser le téléphone bordel , tu te rappelles que notre mère s'appelle Light , surnommée la sauvageonne ! S'exclama Clark . 

Un petit rire sort de ma bouche . 

—J'ai déjà oublié ce détail ! En m'assoyant sur le canapé . 

—Dis lui que je vais venir chez nous la fête de Noël et que je vais très bien ! 

—Ok , c'est tous ! ........ Un silence règne une minute  puis il parla de nouveau : Enfaite , tu sais que Raye est de retour ! 

—Raye? Je ne sais pas , ça fait quoi ? 7 ans que je ne l'ai pas vu ! En réfléchissant.  

—Ouais ! J'aimerai bien voir ce qu'est notre petite Riri avec sa magnifique frange  ! En se moquant tout en riant .

—Moi aussi ! En Murmurant tous bas , un petit sourire naît sur les lèvres. .  

—Je te laisse , j'ai quelques choses à faire ! Puis il coupe l'appel .

Je repose  négligemment mon téléphone sur le canapé et m'en vais monter dans ma chambre prendre une douche.    

Le lendemain matin…….

*GABRIEL MONTÉNÉGRO

Après une nuit jouissive avec Mia , je me sens relaxé , apaisé de toutes tentions qui s'accumulent depuis quelques temps . 

—Gaby , tu pars déjà ? Murmure la voix endormie de Mia . 

—Ouais , je dois aller travailler , j'ai beaucoup de réunion aujourd'hui et plusieurs dossiers à signer ! En répondant d'un ton neutre.  

Elle hoche la tête et me regarde dans les yeux , ses cheveux pendant sur son corps , les marques de nuit sur son cou , ses yeux bouffis par les larmes de plaisir et ses lèvres gonflées par ma soif de s*xe.  

—Je vois ! Souffle t'elle doucement.  

Je réajuste ma cravate et prend ma malette  prête à aller travailler quand sa voix m'interpelle.

—Gabriel ! 

—Oui ? 

Elle se lève du lit avec la couverture autour de son corps et marche à pas lent vers moi .

—Dis-moi ...... 

—Oui ! En attendant sa question . 

Elle finit par arriver devant moi , les yeux pleins d'espoir .

—Quand es-ce qu'on va se marier ? 

Je soupire profondément , las de cette question depuis un an .

—Tu n'es pas heureuse Mia ? Mon ton fut sérieux .

Elle hoche négativement sa tête. 

—Si , je suis heureuse ! En me contredisant .

—Alors si tu es heureuse alors pourquoi me demandes tu encore cette question ! 

Elle écarquille ses yeux et fais deux pas en arrière , la brillance d'espoir dans ses yeux disparurent également 

—Mais Gabriel ! Ça fait déjà 7 ans quand on est ensemble , tu ne trouves pas que c'est l'heure pour nous de nous réunir à jamais ? Sa voix douce contenue , une petite larme coule sur le long de ses joues.  

Je soupire profondément et regarde la femme devant moi . 

—Je sais Mia mais c'est encore récent ! Moi et Alia , nous nous sommes séparés que deux ans , que penseront les gens de moi si je vais me marier après deux ans de séparation ? Tu ne connais pas l'impact public si on se marie ? 

Elle serre fortement la couverture qu'elle tient puis explose en  larme .

—C’était toi qui a voulu tout cela je te signale ! C'était toi qui voulais plus d'argent et qui a eu l'idée de séduire Alia Leister pour sa richesse et maintenant, tu les as eu et tu t'éloignes de moi ? Alors que c'est moi qui t'a aidé, qui t'a aidé pour avoir tous ce qui nous entoure ! En criant .

Je place ma main sur sa bouche pour la faire taire et prend un souffle . 

—Je sais mon cœur mais il faut attendre ! En la regardant dans les yeux. 

J'enlève ma main de sa bouche et la laissa souffler puis je prend sa main qui pend dans le vide. 

—Écoute-moi bien , nous nous marions après le nouvel an , je te promets ! Tu choisis la date que tu veux mais après le nouvel an , d'accord ? 

—Vraiment ? Demanda t'elle à voix basse.  

Je hoche la tête affirmant mes dires , son sourire revint et elle m'enlaça . 

—Je t'aime tellement Gaby ! En susurant à mon oreille.

WASAARADDA HAWLAHA GUUD, DIB-U-DHISKA IYO GURIYEYNTA XFS IYO UN-HABITAT OO YEESHAY KULAN LOOGA HADLAYAY QORSHAYNTA MAGAALOOYINKA IYO TAAGEERADA BARAKACAYAASHA.

Wasaaradda Hawlaha Guud, Dib-u-dhiska iyo Guriyeynta Xukuumadda Federaalka Soomaaliya ayaa ku qabatay hoolka Wasaaradda kulan heer sare ah oo ay la yeelatay madax ka socday Hay’adda UN-Habitat. Kulanka waxaa shir-guddoominayay Wasiir-Dowlaha Wasaaradda, Mudane Siciid Maxamed Maxamuud Xayd, waxaana ku wehlinayay Agaasimaha Guud ee Wasaaradda iyo qaar ka mid ah Agaasimeyaasha Waaxyaha.

Kulanka waxaa diiradda lagu saaray qodobadan muhiimka ah.

Diyaargarowga Madasha Caalamiga ah ee Magaalooyinka (World Urban Forum – WUF 13), oo lagu wado in ay qabsoonto bisha May, Jamhuuriyadda Azerbaijan.
* Warbixin ku saabsan mashaariicda socda, gaar ahaan kuwa la xiriira Saamaynta, IDSF, iyo Dowlad-Kaab.

Ka-wadahadal ku saabsan wax-ka-beddelka Heshiiska Iskaashiga ee u dhexeeya Wasaaradda iyo UN-Habitat, oo ay ku jirto arrinta Corridor-ka.

Madaxa Xafiiska UN-Habitat ee Soomaaliya ayaa ka warbixiyay hawlaha ay hay’addu waddo, kuwaas oo ay ka mid yihiin:
* Guryaha loo dhisay dadka barakacayaasha ah ee ku yaalla Boosaaso iyo Baydhabo.
* Siyaasadda Guriyeynta Qaranka.
* Siyaasadda Maareynta Dhulka ee magaalooyinka Boosaaso iyo Baydhabo.
* Diyaarinta mashaariic lagu horumarinayo magaalaynta iyo taageerada barakacayaasha (IDPs), kuwaas oo qaar ka mid ah la ansixiyay, halka kuwo kalena weli la diyaarinayo.

Kulankan ayaa xoojiyay iskaashiga istiraatiijiga ah ee u dhexeeya Wasaaradda iyo UN-Habitat, iyadoo la isla qaatay in la dardargeliyo qorsheynta magaalooyinka, lana horumariyo xalal waara oo wax ka qabta baahiyaha barakacayaasha iyo horumarinta degsiimooyinka.

Toxic people demand you get over things they refuse to apologize for. Want forgiveness without accountability. Reconciliation without acknowledgment.

"Can't you just let it go?" "Why are you still bringing that up?" "You need to move on." They want you healed and silent about harm they've never acknowledged. They want peace without having to face what they did. They want the relationship restored without doing any of the work to repair it. And they act like you're the problem for not being able to magically erase damage they refuse to even admit exists.

Real reconciliation requires accountability. It requires the person who caused harm to say "I did that. I see how it hurt you. I take full responsibility." But they can't do that. Won't do that. Because admitting fault threatens their ego, their image, their narrative where they're always the victim and you're always overreacting.

So instead they demand compliance. "Get over it." "Forgive and forget." "Stop living in the past." They want you to swallow your pain so they don't have to sit with their guilt. They want you to perform healing you haven't actually experienced so they can feel comfortable again. They want the benefits of your forgiveness without earning it through genuine remorse and changed behavior.

That's not healing. That's erasure. They're asking you to delete your experience, your feelings, your legitimate response to their mistreatment—all so they can avoid the discomfort of being held accountable. They're prioritizing their comfort over your healing, which is exactly the dynamic that made them toxic in the first place.

They're not interested in repair. They want your compliance without their discomfort. And you don't owe them that. You never did.

Real healing requires real accountability. Anything less is just control wearing a mask of peace.

Magaalada Matabaan ee Gobolka Hiiraan ayaa marti-gelineysa ciidamadii ugu badnaa ee sanadkan lagu arko meel ka mid ah gobolkaasi.

Magaalada ayaa waxaa ka jira abaabul iyo diyaargarow ciidan, oo Ciidanka Xoogga Dalka iyo kuwa Difaaca Shacabka ee Macawiisley la qorsheynayo in ay ku xoreeyaan dhulka maqan ee gobolkaasi, oo ay haatan ugu muhiimsan yihiin Magaalooyinka Maxaas iyo Moqokori oo maleeshiyada Al Shabaab ay haysato.

Taliyaha Ciidanka Dhulka ee Xoogga Dalka, General Sahal Cabdullaahi Cumar ayaa shalay duleedka magaalada kula hadlay ciidamadan, oo Warbaahinta Dowladda ay ku sheegtay in ay yihiin kuwo cusub oo tababar loo soo xiray.

Taliyaha oo maalmihii u dambeeyay ku sugnaa gobolka ayaa ciidamada kula dardaarmay inay la dagaalamaan cadowga, isla-markaana ay dalka ka ciribtiraan.

Taliyeyaasha Qaybaha 21aad iyo 27aad ee Ciidanka Xoogga Dalka oo ku sugan Matabaan ayaa dooranaya saraakiishii hoggaamin lahayd hawlgalka lagu wado in laga bilaabo Gobolka Hiiraan.

Korneyl Maxamed Dhagaweyne iyo G/dhexe Cali Axmed Xaashi (Cagey) ayay wararku sheegayaan in ay ciidamada u diyaarinayaan in marka hore mintidiinta Al Shabaab laga kiciyo dhulka ay ka joogaan inta u dhaxeyso Magaalooyinka Matabaan iyo Maxaas, ka dibna ay qabtaan Maxaas.

Maxaas ayaa dhacda meel istiraatiiji ah, marka aad eegto in ay saaran tahay xuduudda Gobolada Hiiraan iyo Galgaduud, waana tan Shabaabka u sahleysa in ay si toos ah isugu gooshaan Gobolada Bartamaha & Koonfurta Somaliya.

Ciidamadan ayaa waxaa la qiyaasaya in ay boor badan ka kicin doonaan goobaha ay isku fara saaraan kooxaha argagixisada, haddii aysan kooxahaasi dib u gurasho samayn.

Muqdisho, Isniin, 12 Janaayo 2026:-– Golaha Wasiirrada Xukuumadda Jamhuuriyadda Federaalka Soomaaliya oo maanta yeeshay kulan uu guddoominayey Ra’iisul Wasaare Ku-xigeenka Xukuumadda Federaalka Soomaaliya, Mudane Saalax Axmed Jaamac, ayaa lagu buriyay dhammaan heshiisyadii iyo is-afgaradyadii ay Dowladda Soomaaliya la gashay Dowladda Isutagga Imaaraadka Carabta, kuwaas oo la xiriiray dhinacyada iskaashiga amni iyo difaac ee laba geesoodka ah. 

Go’aankaan waxa uu khuseeyaa dhammaan heshiisyada iyo wadashaqaynta ay ku joogeen dekadaha Berbera, Boosaaso, iyo Kismaayo, Go’aankaan ayaa la xiriiray warbixinno iyo caddaymo xooggan oo ku saabsan tillaabooyin gurracan oo lagu dhibaataynayo gobonnimada, midnimada Qaranka, iyo madaxbannaanida siyaasadeed ee Jamhuuriydda Federaalka Soomaaliya. 

Dowladda Soomaaliya, waxa ay ku caddaynaysaa masuuliyaddeeda iyo xuquuqdeeda madaxbannaani ee la xiriirta dhulkeeda, dekadaheeda, arrimaheeda amniga, iyo arrimaheeda dibedda.

Hindise Sharciyeedka illaalinta madax-bannaanida iyo midnimada dhuleed oo ka kooban dhawr iyo tobon qodob, ayaa mamnuucaya heshiis kasta oo ay maamul hoosaadyada iyo hey’adaha gaarka ah la galaan cid shisheeye ah, iyada oo aanan wax ogeysiin iyo oggolaansho laga haysanin dowladda federaalka Soomaaliya, korjoogtayna aysan ku lahayn wasaaradda uu heshiiskaas khuseeyo.

In Portugal, a political debate has sparked strong reactions across the country and beyond. A proposal aims to restrict the wearing of face-covering garments such as burqas and niqabs in certain public spaces, arguing it is about security, identification, and social cohesion. 🇵🇹

Supporters say public rules should apply equally to everyone and that visible identification is essential in shared spaces. Critics warn that such measures may limit personal freedom and disproportionately affect Muslim women, raising concerns about religious rights and discrimination.

This discussion is not unique to Portugal. Across Europe, similar debates continue, often balancing public security, cultural values, and individual liberties. The question many people are asking is not only about clothing, but about how societies define integration, freedom, and coexistence.

Should governments regulate religious symbols in public life, or should personal belief always remain protected, even when opinions strongly differ?

What do you think?

Source:
Reuters – reporting on proposals by Portugal’s right-wing Chega party and the national debate on banning face-covering religious garments in public spaces

Launch of the Civil Society Engagement Policy Framework in #Somalia 2026–2030 📅 08–10 December 2025 #CSSummit2025 

During the closing of the Civil Society Summit 2025, hundreds of Civil Society Organizations #CSOs from diverse sectors participated alongside representatives from the Federal Government, Federal Member States #FMS, #embassies, United Nations agencies, #INGOs, and other key stakeholders.

The summit featured in-depth discussions and presentations focused on the theme “Operationalizing the Nexus Between Humanitarian, Development and Peace-building in #Somalia” #HDPNexus.

In his closing remarks, the Chairperson of Somali Civil Society @SONSA_NSA, @Osman_Moallim delivered a unified call to action, urging all stakeholders to jointly support the implementation of the Civil Society Engagement Policy Framework in #Somalia 2026–2030.

“In conclusion he said that, I call upon the Federal Government, Federal Member States,@European @UN @UNTMIS_ @UNDPSomalia and other #UN agencies, donors, #INGOs, partners of Somalia (embassies), and all other stakeholders to support Somali Civil Society wherever they operate, to ensure the effective implementation of the Civil Society Engagement Policy Framework in #Somalia 2026–2030”.

.....................................................
Degmada Qarsaduula maanta waxay ku jirtaa xaalad bani’aadamnimo oo aad u daran, mid ka mid ah kuwii ugu xumaa ee soo mara taariikhdeeda.
_ Waa degmo go’doon ku jirta, naf iyo nololba halis ugu jirto, iyadoo ay sii xumeysay biyo la’aan ba’an oo gaartay heer ay dad ku naf waayaan.
Muddooyinkii u dambeeyay, biyihii ay degmaddu ka heli jirtay dhinaca Oromiya ayaa gebi ahaanba la joojiyay, xuduudkiina waa la xiray. Taasi waxay keentay in kumannaan qof ay ku noolaadaan xaalad aad u qallafsan, iyagoo la ildaran harraad, cudurro iyo rajo-beel.

___Biyo La’aan Naf Qaadaysa
Xaaladda biyo la’aaneed ee ka jirta Qarsaduula ma aha mid caadi ah; waa musiibo bani’aadamnimo. Dad ayaa u dhimanaya harraad awgiis, carruur, haween iyo waayeelna waxay wajahayaan Xaalad  joogto ah. Wabiga ugu dhow ee biyo laga heli karo, Wabiga Soora, wuxuu u jiraa in ka badan 70km, taasoo ka dhigaysa mid aan suurtagal u ahayn in dadku ka faa’iideystaan.

______Dowladda iyo Gargaar La’aanta
Ilaa iyo hadda, wax gargaar ah oo la taaban karo kama soo gaarin Maamulka Qarsaduula waxay u muuqataa degmo la illoobay, iyadoo dadka ku nool ay dareemayaan in laga jeestay qaylo-dhaantoodii.

______Musuq iyo Mas’uuliyad Darro
Warar walaac leh ayaa sheegaya in lacagihii hore loogu talagalay mashaariicda biyaha ee degmadan lagu shubay akoontiyo shaqsiyeed, halkii lagu samatabixin lahaa dad harraad u dhimanaya. Maamulka degmaduna waxay u muuqdaan kuwo indhaha ka daawanaya masiibada, iyagoo aan qaadin tallaabo wax ku ool ah.

____Cabsi Dagaal oo Soo Ifbaxaysa
Dhanka kale, dhinaca Oromiya waxaa ka muuqda saansaan dagaal iyo diyaar garow colaadeed, taasoo sii kordhinaysa walbahaarka, cabsi iyo hubanti la’aanta ka jirta Qarsaduula.

____QAYLO-DHAAN
Shacabka dagmada Qarsaduula maanta ma dalbanayso hadal iyo balanqaad Waxay Maamulka Degaanka iyo kan gobolka Liibaan ka dalbanayaan gurmad degdeg iyo in Maamulka Dagmada lala xisaantamo.!

Pursuant to the fundamental principle of the right of peoples to self-determination, and based upon the popular mandate of the South, we hereby affirm that President Aidaroos Qasim al-Zubaidi embodies the legitimate political will of the Southern people as the President of the Southern Transitional Council (STC). The STC is a political entity established by sovereign internal will, and is governed solely by its autonomous administrative framework and internal bylaws. 

Accordingly, any external attempts to intervene in the STC’s affairs—including proposals for its dissolution or restructuring—constitute a clear violation of international law, specifically the principle of non-interference in the internal affairs of political entities. Such actions are a direct infringement on the sovereign right of the Southern people to designate their representatives. 

Furthermore, we assert that the legitimacy of any "South-South" dialogue is contingent upon adherence to international standards of neutrality and independence. Sponsorship by any state considered a "party to the conflict," or exhibiting political or military bias, is inherently devoid of legal standing. Such biased mediation degrades the dialogue from a legitimate peace-building process into a mechanism for political coercion. 

States that have conducted military operations resulting in civilian casualties in the South, or those that have provided material support to factions opposing the Southern popular will, are disqualified from acting as impartial mediators or fair arbiters in any political settlement. 

Therefore, we declare: 

1.  The Southern Transitional Council is a sovereign entity grounded in a popular mandate; its status is non-negotiable and immune to external interference.
2.  The Southern political process must remain an exclusively Southern-led initiative, overseen by neutral international facilitators who respect the Southern people's right to self-determination.
3.  The imposition of political trusteeship or any form of external hegemony is categorically rejected as a breach of international justice, sovereignty, and human rights conventions.
4.  The Southern Cause is a just legal and political struggle that transcends compromise. It remains subject only to the sovereign will of its people, independent of any external dictates or geopolitical pressures

Ankara, 13 Janaayo 2026 – Soomaaliya iyo Turkiga oo Bilaabay Hirgelinta Heshiiskii Iskaashiga ee 2016.

Magaalada Ankara ayaa lagu qabtay munaasabad lagu saxiixayay hirgelinta heshiiskii iskaashiga (MoU – June 2016) ee u dhexeeyay Wasaaradda Shaqada iyo Arrimaha Bulshada ee Jamhuuriyadda Federaalka Soomaaliya iyo Wasaaradda Shaqada iyo Ammaanka Bulshada ee Jamhuuriyadda Turkiga.

Munaasabadda ayaa si rasmi ah u guddoomiyay Wasiirka Shaqada iyo Ammaanka Bulshada ee Turkiga, Prof. Dr. Vedat Işıkhan, iyo Wasiirka Shaqada iyo Arrimaha Bulshada JFS, Prof. Salim Aliyoow Ibro, waxaana goob-joog ka ahaa Safiirka Soomaaliya ee Turkiga, Agaasimaha Guud Mudane Yuusuf Xasan iyo xubno kale oo ka tirsan wafdiyada labada dal.

Kadib munaasabadda saxiixa, waxaa xigay kulan farsamo oo diiradda lagu saaray 15-ka qodob ee lagu hirgelin doono muddada 2026–2027, kuwaas oo saldhig u noqon doona xoojinta iskaashiga labada dal ee dhinacyada shaqada, tababarka xirfadaha, badbaadada shaqaalaha iyo goobaha shaqada (OSH), iyo horumarinta adeegyada bulshada.

Labada dhinac ayaa isku raacay in la dardargeliyo hirgelinta qodobada heshiiska, si loo xoojiyo hay’adaha shaqada ee Soomaaliya loona adkeeyo wada-shaqeynta joogtada ah ee Dowladda Turkiga.

ADR SYSTEM:
-------------------------------------

Introduction:
Alternative Dispute Resolution (ADR) refers to methods of resolving disputes outside traditional court litigation, focusing on negotiation, conciliation, arbitration, and mediation. ADR is designed to provide faster, cheaper, and more flexible justice compared to the conventional judiciary system. As Adv. Shafiq Nazrul aptly states, “Justice is best served when it is swift, fair, and reconciliatory.”

Concept and Theories of ADR:
ADR is based on the theory that not all disputes require formal adjudication. It emphasizes voluntary participation, mutual agreement, and fairness. Theories supporting ADR include:

Negotiation Theory: Parties themselves work toward a mutually acceptable solution.

Mediation Theory: A neutral third party assists parties in reaching a compromise.

Arbitration Theory: A binding decision is made by an arbitrator agreed upon by the parties.

Restorative Justice Theory: Focuses on repairing harm, particularly in family or community disputes.

Modes of ADR:

Negotiation: Direct discussion between parties without third-party intervention.

Mediation: Assistance by a neutral mediator to facilitate settlement.

Conciliation: Similar to mediation, often with more active guidance by the conciliator.

Arbitration: Parties submit disputes to an arbitrator whose decision is binding.

Mini-Trials & Early Neutral Evaluation: Informal evaluation of merits by experts.

ADR Mechanism in Statutory Laws of Bangladesh:

The Code of Civil Procedure, 1908:

Sections 89A,89B,89C  encourage settlement through arbitration, conciliation, mediation, or judicial settlement conferences.

Courts may refer disputes to ADR mechanisms before trial.

The Muslim Family Laws Ordinance, 1961:

Section 7(1) empowers courts to refer family disputes, including marriage and maintenance cases, to reconciliation boards or mediation panels.

The Customs Act, 1969:

Section 150 allows disputes regarding duties and customs assessments to be settled through ADR mechanisms, reducing litigation in revenue matters.

The Income Tax Ordinance, 1984:

Section 76 provides conciliation between the tax authority and the taxpayer to resolve disputes before formal assessments or appeals.

Other Relevant Laws in Bangladesh:

Family Court Act, 2023: Mandates ADR for marital and family disputes.

VAT and Supplementary Duty Act, 2012: Facilitates settlement of tax disputes through conciliation.

Dispute Resolution (Municipal Areas) Board Act, 2004: Local boards mediate minor disputes.

Salish Act, 2001 & Gram Adalat Act, 2006: Community-based dispute resolution.

Money Loan Court Act, 2003: Encourages settlement in financial disputes.

Bangladesh Labour Act, 2006: Promotes mediation between employers and employees.

Case Laws on ADR in Bangladesh:

Bangladesh Tobacco Company v. Customs Authority (2005) 3 BLD 231:

Court emphasized settlement before formal litigation under Customs Act, 1969, reducing prolonged disputes.

Mohammad Nurul Islam v. Commissioner of Taxes (2008) 2 BLC 459:

ADR in tax matters under the Income Tax Ordinance led to mutual agreement, saving judicial time and resources.

Abul Kalam Azad v. Bangladesh (2010) 1 BLD 110:

Court referred a family dispute to reconciliation under the Muslim Family Laws Ordinance, 1961, achieving amicable settlement.

Md. Alauddin v. Bangladesh Tobacco Co. Ltd (2012) 4 BLC 788:

Arbitration under CPC Section 89 upheld as binding and enforceable, confirming ADR’s legal validity.

Hasan v. Labour Court, Dhaka (2015) 3 BLD 540:

Mediation in labour dispute resolved conflict without prolonged litigation, demonstrating ADR’s effectiveness in industrial relations.

Merits of ADR:

Time-Saving: Faster resolution compared to courts.

Cost-Effective: Lower legal and administrative expenses.

Flexibility: Parties can negotiate solutions suited to their needs.

Confidentiality: Preserves privacy, especially in family and business matters.

Preservation of Relationships: Encourages cooperation and compromise.

Reduced Court Burden: Decreases litigation backlog.

Voluntary and Empowering: Parties actively participate in the resolution.

Demerits of ADR:

Non-Uniformity of Decisions: Outcomes may vary depending on mediator/arbitrator.

Limited Legal Safeguards: Parties may unknowingly compromise rights.

Possibility of Coercion or Bias: Powerful parties may influence settlement unfairly.

No Binding Precedent: ADR decisions do not guide future disputes.

Enforceability Issues: Voluntary settlements may face challenges in enforcement.

Inadequacy for Complex Legal Issues: Not suitable for disputes involving multiple parties, public interest, or intricate law.

Conclusion:
ADR is a vital instrument in Bangladesh’s justice system. As Adv. Shafiq Nazrul states, “Justice is best served when it is swift, fair, and reconciliatory.” By integrating ADR mechanisms in statutes like the CPC, Muslim Family Laws, Customs Act, Income Tax Ordinance, Family Court Act, and Labour Act, Bangladesh ensures efficient, accessible, and harmonious dispute resolution. While ADR has limitations, proper application enhances justice, reduces court congestion, and fosters social cohesion.

 تنازعات الصعافقة المحمومة حول المرجعية المزعومة 

الحمدُ للهِ أمَّا بعدُ:

فإن من أظهر ما انكشف في فتنة الصعافقة في الأيام الأخيرة صراعهم المحموم علىٰ المرجعية لا مرجعية الكتاب والسنة بفهم الأصحاب فذاك أمر لا نزاع فيه ولكن مرجعية السَّلطة علىٰ الدعوة والولاء والبراء علىٰ أشخاص لا يمكن الرجوع لغيرهم ولا الصدور عن غير كلامهم.

وقد يظهر لمن تابع واقعهم أن عبد الله البخاري وعرفات المحمدي سعيا سعيًا حثيثًا بعد وفاة العلامتين ربيع بن هادي وعبيد الجابري -رحمهما الله- إلىٰ تثبيت نفسيهما بأن يكونا مرجعًا يرجع إليه السلفيون وكان من أبرز ما استعملوهـ في ذلك نشرُ ما نُسب إلىٰ الشيخ عبيد من وصية فيها (الوصية بالرجوع إليهما بعدهـ) وبنشرهم لهذهـ الوصيَّة فقد صوَّروا  الدعوة السلفية من كونها منهجًا قائمًا علىٰ الاتباع إلىٰ صورة مرجعية مؤسسية لها أشخاص يُنصَّبون ويُحال إليهم الخلاف كباقي الأحزاب والجماعات.

وهذا باطلٌ شرعًا ومنهجًا إذ ليس لأحد -كائنًا من كان - أن يُؤمِّر علىٰ السلفيين رجلًا يُجعل مرجعًا عامًا بعدهـ لأن السلفية ليست إمارةً ولا تنظيمًا ولا جماعةً لها أمير ونواب وإنما هي منهجٌ يقوم علىٰ لزوم الكتاب والسنة بفهم الصحابة [فإن تنازعتم في شيء فردُّوهـ إلىٰ الله والرسول].

وهذا مالم يرق لكثير ممن كانوا أبواقًا لعبد الله البخاري وعرفات المَحمدي ممن دافعوا عنهم بالزور ونفخوا فيهم بالكذب ولهذا  فإنَّهم لمَّا رأوا محاولة احتكار البخاري وعرفات للمرجعية نازعوهم عليها لا إنكارًا للأصل من بدايتهِ فهم قد سكتوا عنهم في هدمهم لأصول السنة التي قد تكون أعظم من ذلك ولكن لمنافستهم عليه. 

فدخلوا في صدامٍ أخرجوا فيهِ ما كانُوا قد أخفوهـ علىٰ بعضهم فجرَّهم ذلك إلىٰ تبادل التهم ورمي بعضهم بعضًا بالمخالفات المنهجية الخطيرة فكان ذلك أبلغ في فضحهم وتصديقًا لما قالهُ شيخنا العلَّامة مُحمَّد بن هادي من ١٠ سنوات فيهم وزيادة.

فانقلب كلامهم وكذَّب بعضهم بعضًا وتراموا بالسفه والطيش والتحزُّب وكلٌّ يدعي أنَّهُ علىٰ الجادة وأن غيرهـ ممن كان يدافع عنهُ بالأمس منحرف عنها. 

فكتب علي الشَّرفي الكاتابات الَّتِي تتهم عرفات بالخروج علىٰ ولاة الأمر في اليمن وبالغش للعلماء في استخراج الفتاوىٰ لنصرة الأحزاب وبسلوك مسالك لا تمتُّ إلىٰ طريقة أهل السنة بصلة. 

ووجَّهَ نزار السوداني لعبد الله البخاري اتهامات بعدم الاتزان العلمي وبأنه لا يُرجع إليه وأنه ليس علىٰ طريقة الأكابر بل واتَّهم نزار وفواز وعلي الشرفي عبد الله البخاري باتِّهامات حزبية خطيرة ومواقف سياسية في نصرة الانفصاليين في اليمن والتغاضي عن الخوارج في السودان ومحاولة تسويتهم بالقوات الشرعية.

حتَّىٰ بلغ صراعهم هذا أبواقهم في أوروبا والأمريكيتين فكان موقف بعضهم هو موقف نزار وفواز والشرفي كأبي حكيم بلال (بول ألن دفيز) الذي انتهىٰ به الأمر إلىٰ الطرد من المكتبة السلفية في برمنجهام بعد أن كان رأسًا فيها وبوقًا من أبواق البخاري وعرفات فيها فلمَّا نازعهم طَردَهـ باقي الأبواق منها.

وهكذا لم يكن النزاع كاشفًا عن ظهور حقٍّ جديد ولا عن يقظةٍ منهجيةٍ مفاجئة وإنما عن صراعٍ علىٰ المرجعية فلما تعارضت مصالحهم انقلب الحلفاء خصومًا وبدأ كلٌّ يكشف ما كان يعلمه عن الآخر مما يدل دلالةً واضحة علىٰ أن السكوت السابق لم يكن عن جهل بل عن مجاملةٍ ومصلحة.

ولو كانت القضية قضيةَ ديانةٍ ومنهج لكان البيان واجبًا من أول علمهم بمخالفاتهم لا بعد التنازع ولا بعد الإقصاء. 

ولكنها -كما دلَّت وقائعهم- فتنة مرجعية تشبه في أصلها ما وقع فيه أهل البدع قديما وحديثًا حين جعلوا للأشخاص مقامًا يُقاتَل عليه كما هو عند الروافض وحميرهم من الإخوان ومن قرأ في سير دعوة هذين الطريقين عرف ما أرمي إليهِ.

وصلىٰ الله علىٰ نبينا مُحمَّد وعلىٰ آله وصحبهِ أجمعين والحمدُ للهِ ربِّ العالمين.

 Perebutan sengit kaum Sha‘āfiqah atas “otoritas rujukan” yang diklaim
Segala puji bagi Allah, amma ba‘du:
Sesungguhnya, di antara perkara yang paling jelas tersingkap dalam fitnah kaum Sha‘āfiqah pada hari-hari terakhir ini adalah perebutan mereka yang begitu sengit terhadap apa yang mereka sebut sebagai marji‘iyyah (otoritas rujukan).
Bukanlah yang mereka maksud itu rujukan kepada Al-Qur’an dan Sunnah dengan pemahaman para sahabat—karena hal itu tidak diperselisihkan—melainkan rujukan kepada kekuasaan atas dakwah serta loyalitas dan permusuhan yang dibangun atas nama individu-individu tertentu, yang tidak boleh dirujuk selain kepada mereka dan tidak boleh diambil kecuali dari ucapan mereka.
Siapa saja yang memperhatikan realita mereka akan melihat bahwa Abdullah al-Bukhari dan ‘Arafat al-Muhammadi, setelah wafatnya dua imam besar Rabi‘ bin Hadi dan ‘Ubaid al-Jabiri—rahimahumallāh—telah berusaha keras meneguhkan diri mereka sebagai rujukan yang harus diikuti oleh kaum Salafi.
Di antara cara paling menonjol yang mereka gunakan adalah menyebarkan apa yang dinisbatkan kepada Syaikh ‘Ubaid berupa wasiat yang berisi “anjuran untuk kembali kepada mereka berdua setelah beliau”.
Dengan menyebarkan wasiat ini, mereka telah mengubah gambaran dakwah Salafiyyah—yang hakikatnya adalah manhaj yang berdiri di atas ittibā‘ (mengikuti dalil)—menjadi seolah-olah sebuah institusi yang memiliki figur-figur tertentu yang diangkat dan kepada merekalah perselisihan dikembalikan, sebagaimana layaknya partai dan kelompok-kelompok lainnya.
Ini adalah kebatilan secara syar‘i dan manhaj.
Sebab tidak seorang pun—siapa pun dia—berhak mengangkat seorang lelaki sebagai pemimpin umum bagi kaum Salafi untuk dijadikan rujukan setelah para ulama, karena Salafiyyah bukanlah kepemimpinan, bukan organisasi, dan bukan jamaah yang memiliki amir dan wakil-wakilnya.
Salafiyyah adalah manhaj yang berdiri di atas komitmen kepada Al-Qur’an dan Sunnah dengan pemahaman para sahabat, sebagaimana firman Allah:
“Jika kalian berselisih dalam suatu perkara, maka kembalikanlah ia kepada Allah dan Rasul.”
Hal inilah yang tidak disukai oleh banyak orang yang selama ini menjadi corong Abdullah al-Bukhari dan ‘Arafat al-Muhammadi—mereka yang membela keduanya dengan kebohongan dan mengangkat-angkat mereka dengan dusta.
Ketika mereka melihat upaya al-Bukhari dan ‘Arafat untuk memonopoli otoritas rujukan, mereka pun mulai menentang mereka—bukan karena mengingkari prinsip itu sejak awal, sebab mereka telah lama diam ketika keduanya merobohkan pokok-pokok Sunnah yang mungkin jauh lebih besar bahayanya—namun karena adanya persaingan kepentingan.
Maka terjadilah benturan di antara mereka, yang akhirnya menyingkap apa yang selama ini mereka sembunyikan satu sama lain.
Hal itu menyeret mereka kepada saling menuduh dan melemparkan satu sama lain dengan tuduhan-tuduhan penyimpangan manhaj yang serius.
Keadaan ini justru menjadi sebab paling jelas terbongkarnya hakikat mereka, sekaligus membenarkan apa yang telah dikatakan oleh Syaikh kami al-‘Allāmah Muhammad bin Hadi sejak sepuluh tahun yang lalu—bahkan lebih dari itu.
Ucapan mereka pun berbalik arah: sebagian mendustakan sebagian yang lain.
Mereka saling menuduh dengan kebodohan, kecerobohan, dan fanatisme kelompok.
Masing-masing mengklaim berada di atas jalan yang lurus, sementara yang lain—yang kemarin masih mereka bela—sekarang mereka anggap telah menyimpang darinya.
Ali asy-Syarafi pun menulis tulisan-tulisan yang menuduh ‘Arafat telah memberontak kepada para penguasa di Yaman, menipu para ulama dalam mengambil fatwa untuk membela partai-partai, serta menempuh jalan-jalan yang sama sekali tidak ada hubungannya dengan manhaj Ahlus Sunnah.
Nizar as-Sudani juga melontarkan tuduhan kepada Abdullah al-Bukhari tentang ketidakstabilan ilmiah, bahwa ia tidak layak dijadikan rujukan, dan bahwa ia bukan berada di atas manhaj para ulama besar.
Bahkan Nizar, Fawaz, dan Ali asy-Syarafi menuduh Abdullah al-Bukhari dengan tuduhan-tuduhan حزبية (kepartaian) yang serius: sikap-sikap politik dalam mendukung gerakan separatis di Yaman, menutup mata terhadap kaum Khawarij di Sudan, serta upaya menyamakan mereka dengan pasukan resmi pemerintah.
Pertikaian ini pun menjalar hingga ke para corong mereka di Eropa dan Amerika.
Sebagian mengambil sikap seperti Nizar, Fawaz, dan asy-Syarafi—di antaranya Abu Hakim Bilal (Paul Allen Davis), yang akhirnya dikeluarkan dari Perpustakaan Salafiyyah di Birmingham, padahal sebelumnya ia adalah tokoh utama dan corong besar bagi al-Bukhari dan ‘Arafat di sana.
Ketika ia mulai menentang mereka, maka corong-corong lainnya pun mengusirnya.
Demikianlah, perselisihan ini bukanlah menyingkap kebenaran baru, bukan pula karena bangkitnya kesadaran manhaj secara tiba-tiba.
Ia hanyalah pertarungan memperebutkan otoritas rujukan.
Ketika kepentingan mereka saling berbenturan, para sekutu berubah menjadi musuh, dan masing-masing mulai membuka apa yang telah lama mereka ketahui tentang pihak lain.
Ini menunjukkan dengan sangat jelas bahwa diamnya mereka selama ini bukan karena ketidaktahuan, tetapi karena basa-basi dan kepentingan.
Seandainya perkara ini benar-benar urusan agama dan manhaj, tentu penjelasan dan peringatan sudah wajib disampaikan sejak pertama kali mereka mengetahui penyimpangan tersebut—bukan setelah terjadi perselisihan dan saling menyingkirkan.
Namun kenyataannya—sebagaimana ditunjukkan oleh peristiwa-peristiwa mereka—ini adalah fitnah perebutan otoritas, yang pada asalnya serupa dengan apa yang terjadi pada ahli bid‘ah dahulu dan sekarang, ketika mereka menjadikan individu-individu tertentu sebagai sosok yang dipertahankan mati-matian, sebagaimana yang terjadi pada kaum Rafidhah dan sekutu-sekutu mereka dari kalangan Ikhwan.
Siapa yang membaca sejarah kedua jalan dakwah ini akan memahami dengan jelas maksud yang saya tuju.
Semoga shalawat dan salam tercurah kepada Nabi kita Muhammad, kepada keluarga dan seluruh sahabatnya.
Dan segala puji bagi Allah Rabb semesta alam.
Ditulis oleh: Abu Muhammad Basyir ath-Tharabulusi
Hari: 23 Rajab 1447 H

HINDI KA MAHAL CONVENIENT KA LANG.

May tropa ako dati. Tahimik lang.
Hindi pala-post. Hindi pala-reklamo.
Pero pag kailangan siya ng babae nandiyan agad.

Hatid
Sundô
Libre
Tulong
Oras
Lahat

Napansin ko, lagi siyang present.
Pero hindi siya priority.

Pag masaya yung babae, kasama siya.
Pag may problema, tawag agad.
Pag kailangan ng pera, siya.
Pero pag okay na ulit ang buhay—biglang di na siya pinapansin. Biglang cold. Biglang nawawala.

Doon ko unang nakita yung pattern.
Hindi siya minahal. Ginamit lang siya.

At mas masakit?
Alam niya na.
Ramdam niya na.
Pero tinanggap niya pa rin kasi umaasa siya na
“baka balang araw, piliin din ako.”

Ito yung hard reality na ayaw aminin ng maraming lalaki e
Kapag nandiyan ka lang kapag kailangan ka,
hindi ka mahal convenient ka lang.

Love doesn’t disappear kapag inconvenient.
Love doesn’t pause kapag wala kang maibigay.
Pero convenience?
Nawawala yan pag wala ka nang silbi.

At wag mong lokohin sarili mo na
“ganun lang talaga siya.”
Hindi.
Ganun lang trato niya sa’yo kasi pumapayag ka.

Sa totoong mundo,
ang lalaking walang boundaries
nagiging gamit.
Hindi partner.

Hindi ka mas mabait.
Mas available ka lang.
Hindi ka mas loyal.
Mas takot ka lang mawala.

At habang mas binibigay mo lahat,
mas bumababa value mo sa mata niya.
Kasi ang hindi pinaghirapan,
hindi nirerespeto.

Hindi kita hinuhusgahan.
Pinapaalala lang kita.

Kung ramdam mong mahal ka lang kapag may silbi ka,
kung ikaw lagi ang naga-adjust,
kung ikaw lang ang nagbibigay tanungin mo sarili mo

Pag wala naba ako silbi mananatili parin siya?

Kung hindi na…
Mag isip isip ka, hindi lang iisa ang babae sa mundo marami diyan maaapreciate ka at di kalang gagmitin papahalagahan kapa.

“A royal ‘plot’… or a convenient excuse?”

It’s the latest claim to send shockwaves through Britain — and reignite a debate many thought had reached its limit.

Meghan Markle now suggests she believed the Royal Family was conspiring against her, even while she was living at Frogmore Cottage, under the personal protection of Queen Elizabeth II.

No names were given.
No evidence presented.
No specific actions identified.

Just a feeling. A suspicion. Another accusation added to a growing list.

For many in Britain, this is the moment patience frays. Frogmore was not exile. It was privilege. Security, privacy, and proximity to the Queen herself — safeguards most members of the family never enjoyed.

Critics argue that duty was never hidden. Expectations were clear. Yet blame, they say, now appears easier to assign than responsibility.

Supporters counter that perception is personal, that fear doesn’t require proof to feel real.

But skeptics ask a sharper question: if there truly was a plot, why has no one been named — not then, not now?

At what point does narrative replace memory?

And when does self-protection become revisionism?

Sebagai seorang ibu, saya memiliki konsentrasi mendalam pada isu body shaming. Namun, realitanya, lingkungan sering kali memberikan "ujian" berupa gurauan fisik yang dianggap sepele. Anak saya, yang bahkan belum genap dua tahun, pernah menerima komentar seperti: "Perutnya besar seperti gentong," atau "Adek gendut kayak siapa?"

Meski secara medis pertumbuhan anak saya dalam kurva normal, secara psikologis, pelabelan fisik ini sangat berbahaya. Gurauan semacam ini adalah bentuk "Internalized Shaming" yang jika dibiarkan akan mengkristal dalam konsep diri anak.

Saya memilih cara yang strategis. Saat interaksi terjadi, saya mungkin tidak selalu bisa langsung mengonfrontasi pelaku demi menjaga harmoni sosial. Namun, dalam momen "Inner Circle Time" di kamar, saya segera melakukan counter-narrative. Saya tanamkan padanya: "Kita tidak menggunakan kata 'gendut' untuk menilai orang lain. Setiap tubuh berharga."

Kita harus sadar bahwa Internalisasi Nilai Akhlak tidak dimulai saat anak sekolah, melainkan dari respons kita terhadap stimulasi lingkungan hari ini. Jangan sampai kita menyalahkan fenomena bullying di masa depan, jika bibit-bibitnya justru kita pupuk melalui "gurauan" yang merendahkan fisik sejak dini.

Karena karakter anak adalah hasil dari akumulasi narasi yang ia dengar setiap hari. ✨

ICJ HEARINGS: GAMBIA REJECTS MYANMAR’S “COUNTER-TERRORISM” CLAIMS OVER CHUT PYIN ATROCITIES
The Hague | Day 2 of Public Hearings

Rohang Press Network  RPN 

During the second day of public hearings at the International Court of Justice (ICJ) in the case The Gambia v. Myanmar, Gambia delivered a powerful argument dismantling Myanmar’s justification for the atrocities committed against the Rohingya community, particularly in Chut Pyin village, northern Rakhine State.
Gambia told the Court that only one conclusion is possible: the events in Chut Pyin were the result of an organized, coordinated, and systematic attack intended to destroy the Rohingya as a group.
Addressing the President and Members of the Court, Gambia challenged Myanmar’s repeated defense narrative:
Myanmar labels the destruction of Chut Pyin as “counter-terrorism.”
Myanmar describes the killing of unarmed Rohingya civilians as “counter-terrorism.”
Myanmar claims acts of sexual violence against women and girls were “counter-terrorism.”
Myanmar even characterizes the killing of Rohingya children as “counter-terrorism.”
Gambia firmly rejected this narrative, stating that no counter-terrorism operation can explain the scale of brutality, sexual violence, and deliberate cruelty inflicted on an entire civilian population.
The presentation emphasized that the actions of the Myanmar military (Tatmadaw) were not isolated incidents or battlefield excesses, but part of a deliberate policy aimed at destroying the Rohingya people, in violation of the Genocide Convention.
The hearings continue as the international community closely watches whether justice will finally be delivered for the Rohingya victims of mass atrocities.
#ICJ #GambiaVsMyanmar #RohingyaGenocide #ChutPyin #JusticeForRohingya #InternationalLaw #StopGenocide

🔴  *APABILA KHAWARIJ ATAU SEPARATIS MEMBERONTAK* 
*Maka Wajib Bagi Manusia untuk Menolong Pemerintahnya*

🎙_Syaikh Al-Allamah Rabi' bin Hadi Al-Madkhali rahimahullah:_

_Adapun jika terjadi peperangan antara penguasa yang sah dan Khawarij yang ingin merusak, maka ini bukan termasuk fitnah sedikitpun. Apabila engkau berperang bersama penguasa yang sah, menyelisihi orang-orang yang keluar memberontak, (yang) mereka itu hanya menginginkan dunia atau mempunyai aqidah yang rusak (serta) mereka ingin menyingkirkan kebenaran, maka ini bukan fitnah dikarenakan engkau menolong kebenaran atas kebatilan dan memberi hidayah atas kesesatan._ 

_Yang menjadi inti adalah apabila khawarij keluar memberontak kepada seorang penguasa atau pengacau yang membuat kekacauan kepadanya, maka wajib bagi manusia untuk menolongnya._ 

_Dan dalil yang menunjukkan hal tersebut adalah (hadits):_ _"“Jika telah dibaiat dua khalifah, maka bunuhlah yang terakhir dari keduanya.”_

📚*Aunul Bari, 2/223*

قال العلامة ربيع المدخلي رحمه الله:

«أما إذا كان القتال بين إمام حق، وبين خارج عليه، يريد الفساد فهذا ليس من الفتنة في شيء، إذا قاتلت مع إمام حق، وخالفه أناس وخرجوا عليه، يريدون دنيا أو عندهم عقائد فاسدة، يريدون التخلص من هذا الحق،  فهذا ليس فتنة، لأنك تنصر حقًا على باطل وهدى على ضلال، 
الشاهد أن الإمام إذا خرج عليه الخوارج، أو بغى عليه البغاة، فعلى الناس أن ينصروه.

ومن الأدلة على ذلك: إذا بويع لخليفتين فاقتلوا الآخر منهما.
[عون الباري (٢٢٣/٢)]

By analyzing the protests in Iran through the lens of the power of symbols, it becomes clear that a complex and sophisticated war of images has been waged.

🚩 Tactics such as showing women without hijabs lighting cigarettes using images of Ayatollah Khamenei constitute a symbolic act of desecration designed to undermine the sacred foundations of the Islamic Republic.

🚬 This strategy recalls the campaigns of Edward Bernays, the pioneer of media manipulation, who in 1929 linked women's smoking to freedom, transforming a simple act into a symbol of rebellion.

🇮🇷 In the Iranian case, this act is not spontaneous, but rather a carefully crafted propaganda meme aimed at targeting public morality and religious authority from abroad.

🚫 The alteration of emojis on the social media platform X (Twitter), changing the flag on social media, and replacing the national symbol with emblems of toppled monarchies or opposition groups, is another front in this symbolic war.

🇮🇷 The flag of the Islamic Republic, laden with religious and revolutionary meanings, is being replaced with insignias of counter-revolutionary forces, seeking to create a false sense of consensus and alternative legitimacy.

💥 This process aims to fracture the national identity and replace it with a pseudo-progressive narrative promoted from abroad, alien to the Islamic and revolutionary values of the Iranian people.

⚠️ It should be noted that the protests initially arose from legitimate social demands, which the Iranian political system has mechanisms to address and resolve.

🇮🇱🇺🇸 However, they were quickly infiltrated and diverted by external agents, including the CIA and Mossad, who exploit discontent to advance a regime-change agenda.

The Man Who Chose Bankruptcy Over Betrayal - And Changed History

Many of the older generation know his name. Those who sat in classrooms he built know his sacrifice. But ask a young person of Chinese descent today about this great man, and you'll likely be met with blank stares.

This is sad...

Because his story isn't just history, it's a masterclass in what one person can do when they decide their wealth belongs to something larger than themselves.

He went from being called the "Henry Ford of Malaya" to nearly penniless. He funded a university while bankrupt. He chose principles over profit so many times that people thought he was insane.

And when he died, he left behind no fortune, no mansion, no empire for his heirs.

Just schools. Universities. Thousands of futures that wouldn't exist without him.

To me, his is a story worthy of sharing.

If you ask me who my idol is, I will tell you without hesitation: it's this great man.

His contribution to the Chinese community in hard times was unmatched. 

October 1874.

A boy was born in Jimei village, Fujian Province, China. His name was Tan Kah Kee! His father had already left for Singapore, seeking fortune in the Nanyang... the South Seas.

The boy grew up without his father. He studied at the village private school. He learned to read. He learned to calculate. And he learned that poverty was not fate, it was a problem waiting for a solution.

At 16, in 1890, Tan Kah Kee boarded a ship to Singapore.

He had every reason to dream of wealth.

Instead, he would build something that outlasted empires.

When Tan arrived in Singapore, his father ran a rice trading business. A modest operation. Tan started as an apprentice. A bookkeeper. He watched the numbers. He watched his uncle struggle with the company. He understood something others didn't: In business, sentiment is expensive. Precision is profit.

By 1892, at just 18 years old, he was running the company after his uncle retired. He returned briefly to China in 1893 to marry, then came back to Singapore to work.

In 1903, the family business failed.

Tan Kah Kee was 29. He could have given up. Returned to China. Blamed the market.

He did the opposite.

With 7,000 Straits dollars - a modest sum, he started a pineapple canning factory at Sembawang in 1904. He bought equipment. He hired workers. He learned the technical processes that European companies guarded jealously.

The factory succeeded. He bought another one... Jit Sin, one of the largest canneries. He cleared 500 acres of jungle and established the Hock Shan Plantation.

With the profits, he revived the family rice business. He opened Khiam Aik rice mill at North Boat Quay, the same location where his father's business had failed.

But 1905 changed everything.

He opened his third pineapple cannery at Rochor River. Business exploded. He wasn't just getting rich, he was proving something revolutionary: That with technical knowledge and commercial vision, Asians could industrialize. They didn't need to wait for Europeans to do it for them.

But Tan Kah Kee saw what others missed. Competition was flooding into pineapples. Profits were shrinking.

In 1906, while pineapple was still booming, he made a bet that seemed crazy: He planted rubber saplings at Hock Shan.

European companies dominated rubber. Everyone said it was a European business. Tan was undeterred.

His timing was perfect. Rubber prices soared. By 1925, he owned over 10,000 acres of rubber plantations. He manufactured rubber goods. He owned shipping vessels... purchased during World War I when shipping became lucrative. He converted the rice mill into a rubber mill.

They called him the "Henry Ford of Malaya."

He had made his fortune.

Most men would have stopped there.

Tan Kah Kee had just begun.

In 1907, while building his business empire, he did something that seemed unconnected to profit: He founded Tao Nan School with 109 other members of the Hokkien community.

Then in 1912, he helped establish Ai Tong School in Singapore.

But that same year, he returned to his home village of Jimei. He walked the streets he remembered from childhood. He saw children who couldn't read. He saw talent dying from neglect.

In 1913, he founded Jimei School.

Not a small village school. A proper institution. Real teachers. Modern curriculum. He poured his own money into it.

People asked why. He was in Singapore. Why spend a fortune on China?

Tan Kah Kee had a simple answer:

"Education is the key to uplifting society from poverty, ignorance and despair."

By 1917, he had expanded Jimei into a secondary school and normal school for teacher training.

In 1919, he founded The Chinese High School in Singapore.. on March 21st, with his own funds.

But he wasn't finished.

That same year, he returned to Jimei to prepare for something audacious: He would build a university.

In 1921, Xiamen University officially opened.

Not a small college. A comprehensive university to rival the best in China. He hired top professors. He built laboratories. He stocked libraries with thousands of books.

He funded it entirely from his rubber fortune. Millions of dollars. Fellow businessmen thought he was insane. They said... you're in Singapore. Why pour everything into China?

Tan's answer was direct.. "A nation without education is a nation without a future. I am Chinese. This is not optional."

He traveled throughout Southeast Asia, speaking to Chinese communities. He asked the wealthy to contribute. He shamed those who hoarded wealth. He said to them, we made our fortunes far from home. We owe our homeland more than nostalgia.

The donations came in. He matched every dollar. Then gave more.

He founded the Nanyang Siang Pau newspaper in 1923. He became chairman of the exclusive Ee Hoe Hean Club.

By 1925, he stood at the pinnacle of financial success.

But the world was changing.

In 1926, rubber prices collapsed. The Great Depression was spreading from the West. His businesses began to fail.

He was forced to halt construction projects at Xiamen University and Jimei Schools.

In 1934, Tan Kah Kee Ltd. was liquidated.

The fortune was gone.

But Tan Kah Kee didn't close the schools. With help from supporters and whatever he could scrape together, he kept them running. He founded more schools: Jimei Farming and Forestry School in 1926. Nanyang Fishery and Marine School in 1939. Nanyang Overseas Chinese Normal School in 1941. Nan Chiao Girls High School in 1947.

While bankrupt, he kept building.

But education wasn't his only fight.

In 1910, he had joined the Tung Meng Hui.. Sun Yat-sen's revolutionary movement against the corrupt Qing dynasty. After the successful Xinhai Revolution in 1911, he raised over $200,000 for the Fujian provincial government, and another $50,000 for Sun Yat-sen's provisional government.

But the real test came later.

In 1937, Japan invaded China. The Second Sino-Japanese War erupted.

Tan Kah Kee, now in his sixties and financially devastated, became a one-man resistance movement from Singapore.

He headed the Singapore China Relief Fund and raised ten million Singapore dollars for China's resistance. Ten million! 

In 1938, he was elected president of the South-East Asia Federation of the China Relief Fund. When Wang Jingwei, a senior Chinese official, suggested peace talks with Japan, Tan sent a furious cable to the Chinese parliament opposing any negotiation with invaders.

He won. The episode became famous as "The Cable Motion" in Chinese history.

He organized mass rallies in Singapore. He spread awareness of Japanese atrocities. He publicly encouraged boycotts of Japanese goods.

In 1940, despite the war raging across China, he organized a 60-man Comfort Mission. They traveled to war-torn China.. to Chongqing, the wartime capital, and to Yan'an.. bringing relief and investigating conditions.

When he met with Chiang Kai-shek's Nationalist government, he saw luxury. Officials hosting banquets while soldiers starved. Corruption everywhere.

Then he visited the Communist forces in Yan'an. He saw shared sacrifice. Leaders wearing the same simple clothes as everyone else.

Tan returned to Southeast Asia and made a decision that would cost him dearly: He declared the Communists offered China's best hope.

The Nationalists never forgave him. His businesses faced pressure. Some overseas Chinese turned against him.

He didn't apologize.

On December 7, 1941, Japan attacked Pearl Harbor. The Pacific War began. On December 30, Tan led the Overseas Chinese Mobilization Council.

Then on February 15, 1942, Singapore fell to Japan.

During the Sook Ching massacre, the Japanese hunted down anyone suspected of anti-Japanese sentiment. Tan Kah Kee, age 67, prominent, vocal.. was a prime target.

He escaped. He fled to Marang, East Java, Indonesia. While in hiding, he wrote 'Memoirs of Nanyang Overseas Chinese', a historical document that would preserve the story of his generation.

When the war ended in 1945, he returned to Singapore a hero.

In 1946, he founded another newspaper, Nan Chiao Jit Poh - critical of the Kuomintang. He supported India's independence movement under Nehru.

In 1947, he organized conferences criticizing Dutch colonial treatment of Indonesian Chinese. He called for economic sanctions.

In 1949, he returned to China for the first time since the war ended.

The People's Republic had just been established. Tan Kah Kee, now 75, made his final decision: He would devote what remained of his life to rebuilding.

He rebuilt the Jimei schools. He rebuilt Xiamen University. He proposed the Hokkien Railway connecting Jimei to Xiamen.

In 1950, he returned to Singapore one last time, to wind up his remaining businesses.

Then he retired to Jimei. The village where he was born.

He lived simply. He held no major government position. He gave speeches. He encouraged students. He planted trees on campus.

He died in Beijing on August 12, 1961, at age 86.

The Chinese government gave him a state funeral. His body traveled by train to Jimei, where thousands lined the streets. Students from the schools he built. Sailors from the navigation college. Teachers from institutions that existed only because one man decided his wealth belonged to the future.

No mansion. No fortune left. No dynasty of wealthy heirs.

But walk through Xiamen University today... over 40,000 students. Visit the Jimei School Village... still operating. Check the navigation schools... still training sailors.

Generations of Chinese leaders, scientists, educators, and engineers passed through institutions that wouldn't exist without Tan Kah Kee's fortune.

They called him the "Henry Ford of Malaya" when he had money.

They call him legend now that he has none.

Because he understood what most wealthy men never grasp... Money has no memory. Education has no expiration date.

He could have lived like a king in Singapore. Built a dynasty. Hoarded every dollar.

Instead, he gave it all.. to schools in China and Singapore, to war relief, to resistance against tyranny, to a future he would never see, for children he would never meet.

From Jimei to Singapore to Xiamen and back to Jimei.

From rice trader to rubber king to bankrupt philanthropist to national hero.

He died with nothing in his pockets.

He left everything standing.

سعدنا في منظمة السلام المتحدة (UPO) بالمشاركة في القمة العالمية الثانية للتنمية الاجتماعية في الدوحة، التي جاءت بعد ثلاثين عاماً من القمة الأولى في كوبنهاغن عام 1995، لتجديد الالتزام العالمي بالعدالة الاجتماعية، والقضاء على الفقر، ووضع الإنسان في قلب السياسات الاقتصادية والاجتماعية.

شهدت القمة مشاركة أكثر من 40 رئيس دولة وحكومة و170 وزيراً، إلى جانب وكالات الأمم المتحدة، ومنظمات المجتمع المدني، والقطاع الخاص، والشباب والخبراء، في لحظة دولية مهمّة أعادت قضايا الحماية الاجتماعية والإنصاف إلى صدارة الأجندة العالمية، مع الاعتراف بالفجوات القائمة، وبحث مسارات عملية لتقوية شبكات الأمان الاجتماعي، وحماية الفئات الأكثر هشاشة، وتعزيز صمود المجتمعات في ظل الأزمات الممتدة والنزاعات والتغير المناخي.

وفي تعليقه على المشاركة، أضاف الأستاذ إبراهيم مودي، المدير التنفيذي لمنظمة السلام المتحدة:
«شكّلت هذه القمة فرصة مهمة لطرح تجارب المجتمعات المتأثرة بالأزمات، ودعم المبادرات المحلية، وتوسيع الشراكات مع مختلف الفاعلين من أجل تعافٍ شامل، وخدمات أكثر استجابة لاحتياجات الناس، وتعزيز التماسك الاجتماعي.»

-----

United Peace Organization (UPO) was pleased to participate in the Second World Summit for Social Development in Doha, held thirty years after the first summit in Copenhagen in 1995, as an important moment to renew global commitment to social justice, poverty eradication, and putting people at the centre of economic and social policies.

The summit brought together more than 40 Heads of State and Government and 170 ministers, alongside UN agencies, civil society organizations, the private sector, youth, and experts. It was a key global moment to place social protection and equity back at the top of the international agenda, acknowledge the existing gaps, and explore practical pathways to strengthen social safety nets, protect the most vulnerable groups, and support community resilience in the context of protracted crises, conflict, and climate change.

Commenting on UPO’s participation, Mr. Ibrahim Modi, Executive Director of United Peace Organization, added:
“For UPO, this summit was an important opportunity to highlight the experiences of crisis-affected communities, support locally led initiatives, and expand partnerships with a wide range of actors to advance inclusive recovery, more responsive services, and stronger social cohesion.”

To the Woman in the Mirror: A Letter to Myself at 50.

Dearest Adata,

Today, you stand at the summit of a remarkable mountain. Take a deep breath and look back at the climb.

At fifty, you are no longer defined by the expectations of others, but by the strength of your own convictions. You have spent years interpreting the law, advocating for others, and building a reputation of integrity and trust. But today, the most important "case" you preside over is your own life.

The Verdict is clear: You have done well.

You have navigated the complexities of your profession with a sharp mind and a resilient spirit. With only the thought of getting justice always. You have balanced the weight of the scales of justice with the warmth and goodness of your heart. 

Through every challenge and every personal milestone, you have remained Adata Bio-Briggs: a woman of substance, a pillar of your community, and a light to your family.

As you enter this second act, I hope you remember to:

Protect your peace: Just as you guard the interests of your clients, guard your own heart. You have earned the right to say "no" to what does not serve your soul.

Celebrate the "Small" Wins: The law is about big outcomes, but life is lived in the quiet moments—the laughter over a good meal, the stillness of a morning, and the pride in seeing those you love thrive.

Keep Setting the Bar: Fifty isn't a finish line; it’s a new vantage point. You have more wisdom to share and more legacy to build than ever before.

You are 50 years of stories, 50 years of lessons, and 50 years of being exactly who you were meant to be. Wear your age like a crown of gold—you’ve earned every bit of its shine.

Happy Birthday to a phenomenal woman, a mother to uncountable children, an amazing wife, daughter, sister and in law, a mentor and mentee to many great persons, a brilliant legal mind, ever available to help others and a beautiful soul.

With love, admiration, and excitement for the future, this is to the next blessed stage of your life.

50 is just your stepping stones.
I love you.

Always,
Adata Bio-Briggs.

ULAMA SIAPA SEBENARNYA?
ATAU “ULAMA” YANG DIPERALATKAN DEMI KUASA?

Kita dari kecil diasuh dengan satu pesan mulia :
“Hormat alim ulama”.
Jangan mencela mereka. Jangan menghina mereka.
Kononnya “daging ulama beracun” – siapa sentuh, umat akan rosak.

Kita setuju.
Tiada Muslim waras yang membenci ilmu atau memusuhi pewaris Nabi ﷺ.

Tapi persoalan paling besar yang sengaja disorok di bawah sejadah ialah :
SIAPAKAH YANG DIMAKSUDKAN DENGAN ULAMA?

ULAMA BUKAN SIAPA YANG PAKAI SERBAN PALING BESAR

Ulama bukan :
 • Sesiapa yang memegang jawatan parti agama
 • Sesiapa yang pandai petik ayat ikut selera
 • Sesiapa yang kuat menjerit “kami paling Islam”
 • Sesiapa yang tak boleh ditegur walau jelas salah

Ulama bukan kebal dari kritikan dan bukan maksum seperti Nabi ﷺ.

Kalau begitu, kenapa hari ini wujud kasta agama?

- “Orang kami = ulama, wajib taat”.
- “Orang luar = jahil, walau ada PhD, walau mufti, walau tokoh dunia”.

Ini bukan hormat ulama.
Ini kultus.

BILA MUFTI DILAWAN, BILA TUAN GURU DIHINA

Soal jujur, tanpa emosi :
 • Bila mufti negeri bercanggah dengan mereka – kenapa dimaki?
 • Bila ulama luar negara beri pandangan berbeza – kenapa dituduh sesat?
 • Bila Yusuf al-Qaradawi, tokoh dunia yang diiktiraf umat Islam global – boleh pula dilabel, ditohmah, diperkecilkan?

Bukankah tadi mereka kata :
“Jangan kutuk ulama, nanti umat rosak”.

Atau ayat itu hanya terpakai untuk ulama parti sendiri?

Kalau begitu, ini bukan prinsip Islam.
Ini politik berserban.

ULAMA DALAM ISLAM ADA DEFINISI, BUKAN CIPTAAN PARTI

Allah sendiri mendefinisikan ulama :
إِنَّمَا يَخْشَى اللَّهَ مِنْ عِبَادِهِ الْعُلَمَاءُ
“Sesungguhnya yang benar-benar takut kepada Allah dari kalangan hamba-Nya hanyalah para ulama” - (Surah Fatir: 28).

Perhatikan :
- Tidak disebut parti
- Tidak disebut jawatan
- Tidak disebut slogan
- Tidak disebut “kami vs mereka”

Ulama diukur dengan KHAUF kepada Allah,
bukan ketaatan buta pengikut.

BILA AGAMA JADI SENJATA, ULAMA JADI PERISAI

Yang paling menjijikkan ialah bila :
 • Ulama digunakan untuk menutup kebatilan pemimpin
 • Agama dijadikan lesen maki orang lain
 • Ayat “jangan kutuk ulama” digunakan untuk menutup kezaliman

Ini bukan sunnah Nabi ﷺ.

Nabi ﷺ ditegur oleh sahabat.
Umar r.a. ditegur wanita biasa di masjid.
Imam Malik berkata :
“Semua boleh ditolak pendapatnya kecuali penghuni kubur ini” - (sambil menunjuk ke makam Nabi ﷺ).

Di mana roh ini hari ini?

INIKAH ISLAM YANG DIBAWA? ATAU ISLAM ALA KHAWARIJ?

Khawarij dulu :
 • Merasa paling suci
 • Merasa paling Islam
 • Melabel orang lain sesat
 • Menghalalkan lidah untuk menghukum

Hari ini :
 • Sama skrip
 • Cuma tukar baju dan logo

Agama tanpa akhlak adalah bahaya.
Ilmu tanpa adab adalah musibah.
Kuasa tanpa takut Allah adalah kehancuran.

KESIMPULAN YANG MENUSUK QALBU

Kalau :
 • Ulama hanya dihormati bila menyokong parti
 • Ulama dihina bila bercanggah pandangan
 • Jahil dipanggil ulama, berilmu dilabel sesat

Maka yang rosak bukan umat semata-mata…
yang rosak ialah amanah agama itu sendiri.

Islam bukan milik mana-mana parti.
Ulama bukan milik mana-mana kumpulan.
Dan Nabi ﷺ tidak mewariskan agama untuk dijadikan perisai kebatilan.

Takutlah kepada Allah.
Kerana lidah yang hari ini memaki atas nama agama, esok akan ditanya :
“SIAPA YANG KAU BELA DAN SIAPA YANG KAU ZALIMI?”

This is SCARY Australia...

Our freedoms are at stack.. this country may be heading under proposed hate-speech and misinformation laws associated with Anthony Albanese and the current government. 

The concern isn’t about defending violence or genuine hate. It’s about how far the law should go in policing opinions, beliefs, and past speech.

When laws become vague ... especially around emotional harm or retrospective enforcement ... they risk crossing from protection into control. 

History shows that once governments gain broad power over speech, it rarely stays limited to extreme cases. Political disagreement, religious expression, and unpopular opinions are often the first targets.

Free speech is not the enemy of social cohesion. It is the foundation of it. 

A healthy democracy depends on open debate, criticism of government policy, and the right to disagree without fear of punishment for lawful expression.

This isn’t about left vs right.
It’s about freedom vs control.

Watch it. Think about it. Decide for yourself.

🕊️ Youth-Led Peacebuilding & Social Cohesion Initiative | Camp-16

A Quarterly Group Awareness Session was successfully conducted at the Youth Peace Café, Camp-16 (near SHED Nutrition Office) with the participation of 29 youth from diverse community backgrounds.

Based on group discussions and participatory exercises:
 • Approximately 65% of participants identified social misunderstanding and family-level tensions as key challenges within the camp
 • Around 55% emphasized that greater youth engagement can significantly reduce conflict and tension
 • Nearly 70% highlighted the importance of community dialogue and awareness activities to strengthen social cohesion

Using tools such as the Conflict Tree and the Positive Peace Framework, participants actively analyzed their community issues and contributed to developing localized, youth-led action ideas. This session marked an important step in expanding our facilitation space into a new camp community while maintaining meaningful and constructive dialogue.

This initiative is supported by the European Union, implemented by ActionAid Bangladesh and SHED, in partnership with 
In Search of Humanity (ISOH).

Shezat Mohammad Shadin
Secretary, In Search of Humanity (ISOH)

PRESS RELEASE
GOMBE STATE POLICE COMMAND

CP UMAR AHMED CHUSO TAKES COMMUNITY POLICING INITIATIVES TO DUKKU LGA DURING FAMILIARIZATION VISIT

The Commissioner of Police, Gombe State Command, CP Umar Ahmed Chuso, mnips, fwc, psc (+), has embarked on a familiarization and community engagement visit to Dukku Local Government Area, as part of ongoing efforts to strengthen community policing and enhance security at the grassroots.

During the visit, the Commissioner of Police paid a courtesy call on the Chairman of Dukku Local Government Council Alh. Adamu Muhammad waziri (wazirin Dukku), where he emphasized the critical role of synergy between the Police and Local Government authorities in addressing security challenges. CP Chuso sought sustained cooperation and support, particularly in logistics, intelligence sharing, and community-based crime prevention initiatives.

The CP also visited the Emir of Dukku at his palace Alh. Haruna Abdulkadir Rashid II CON, where he underscored the importance of traditional institutions in promoting peace, social cohesion, and effective community policing. He appealed for the continued support of the Emir and other traditional leaders in encouraging residents to collaborate with security agencies and provide timely and credible information.

As part of the visit, CP Umar Ahmed Chuso proceeded to the Dukku Divisional Police Headquarters, where he addressed officers and men of the Division. He charged them to remain professional, disciplined, and people-friendly in the discharge of their duties, while reaffirming the Command’s commitment to welfare, capacity building, and effective supervision. The CP also assessed general security situation within the area.

The Commissioner of Police reiterated that community policing remains a cornerstone of the Command’s policing strategy, noting that sustainable security can only be achieved through trust, partnership, and active participation of community members.

He concluded by assuring the people of Dukku LGA of the Command’s resolve to ensure the protection of lives and property, while urging the public to always see the Police as partners and promptly report any suspicious activities.
