মহামান্য হাইকোর্ট নাকি রায় দিয়েছেন, প্রথম স্ত্রীর অনুমতি ছাড়াই দ্বিতীয় বিয়ে করা যাবে! কথাটি আংশিক সত্য, কিন্তু পুরো সত্য নয়।

👉চলুন সহজভাবে আসল বিষয়টি বুঝে নিই। বাংলাদেশে দ্বিতীয় বিবাহ নিয়ন্ত্রণকারী মূল আইন হলো- মুসলিম পারিবারিক আইন অধ্যাদেশ, ১৯৬১। এই আইন গত ৬০ বছরেরও বেশি সময় ধরে অপরিবর্তিতভাবে কার্যকর রয়েছে।

আইনের মূল কথা হলো-
👉আইনে প্রথম স্ত্রীর অনুমতিকে সরাসরি বাধ্যতামূলক শর্ত হিসেবে উল্লেখ করা হয়নি।
👉তবে সালিশি পরিষদ (Arbitration Council)-এর পূর্ব অনুমতি নেওয়া বাধ্যতামূলক।
👉সালিশি পরিষদের অনুমতি ছাড়া দ্বিতীয় বিবাহ করলে তা আইন লঙ্ঘন হিসেবে গণ্য হবে এবং স্বামী দণ্ডনীয় অপরাধে অভিযুক্ত হবেন।

মহামান্য হাইকোর্টের সাম্প্রতিক রায় কী বলেছে❓
আদালত কোনো নতুন আইন তৈরি করেনি। বরং ১৯৬১ সালের বিদ্যমান আইনের সঠিক ব্যাখ্যা পুনরায় স্পষ্ট করেছে যে-
👉দ্বিতীয় বিবাহের অনুমোদনের আইনগত কর্তৃত্ব সালিশি পরিষদের হাতে।
👉স্ত্রীর অনুমতি অবশ্যই লাগবে, এটি সামাজিকভাবে প্রচলিত ধারণা ছিল,কিন্তু আইনের ভাষায় বিষয়টি এভাবে সরাসরি লেখা নেই।

সুতরাং বাস্তব চিত্র হলো-
নতুন করে কোনো আইন তৈরি হয়নি।
 স্ত্রীর অধিকার বাতিল হয়নি।
১৯৬১ সালের আইনই বহাল আছে।
সালিশি পরিষদের অনুমতি এখনো বাধ্যতামূলক
ভুল তথ্য ছড়ালে বিভ্রান্তি বাড়ে, আর সঠিক আইন জানলে অধিকার সুরক্ষিত থাকে। আইন বুঝে সিদ্ধান্ত নিন, গুজবে কান দিবেন না।

The Report That Broke the Family

My phone buzzed halfway through a double shift, and I almost didn’t look. I was wiping down the counter at the cafe, sticky syrup coating my fingers, when the notification lit up the screen. It was from my mother, and it didn’t say “Happy Birthday.”

We sold your car. Family comes first. Be grateful we let you live here.

No cake. No party. No emoji softening the blow. Just my entire independence gone in one text message.

I’m Ava. I’m twenty-two years old. And that car—a modest Honda hatchback with a dent in the passenger door and seats that smelled faintly of the coconut air freshener I’d bought to mask the previous owner’s cigarette habit—was the one thing I’d worked two years to buy. Every early morning, every late night, every smile forced through exhaustion for demanding customers had gone toward that car.

Before I could process the first message, another arrived: Your brother’s starting college. You’ll cover his first semester. $6,000. It’s due this week.

They’d written it like a bill, like I was an ATM with a pulse and a duty to dispense on demand.

I stared at the screen, my chest burning with something too hot to be just anger and too sharp to be just hurt. Then I typed one word back: No.

A second later, my phone rang. I answered, and my father’s voice exploded through the speaker before I could say hello.

“Pack your stuff. You’re dead to us. You don’t get to say no after everything we’ve done for you.”

The line went dead. The cafe noise around me—the hiss of the espresso machine, the murmur of conversations, the clatter of dishes—faded into background static. On my birthday, my car was gone. My money was already spent in their calculations. And apparently, I was dead for daring to push back.

Chapter 1: The Last Time

When my shift ended six hours later, there was no car waiting for me in the lot. Just empty asphalt and the realization that they had actually done it. I walked the three miles home in the humid Florida heat, replaying every time I’d fixed their Wi-Fi, set up their online accounts, paid something “just this once” because they’d made it sound temporary.

If I was so disposable, so easy to cut off, then so were all the little ways I’d been keeping their lives running.

The house smelled the same as always when I stepped through the front door that night—a mix of my mother’s vanilla candles and the faint mustiness of old carpet that no amount of air freshener could mask. I stood in the entryway, duffel bag still over my shoulder, and asked the question I already knew the answer to.

“Did you really sell my car?”

My mother, Linda, was at the kitchen counter scrolling through her phone like nothing catastrophic had just happened. She didn’t even look up when she answered.

“We did what we had to do. Family comes first. Your brother’s future comes before your little convenience.”

My little convenience. Two years of double shifts in a sticky, noisy cafe reduced to “convenience” because it didn’t fit her narrative of noble sacrifice.

“You didn’t pay for it,” I said, my voice shaking despite my effort to stay calm. “I did. Every dollar.”

My father, Gary, rounded the corner from the living room, the television still blaring some football game behind him. “You live under our roof,” he snapped. “You eat our food. You don’t get to throw a tantrum because we made a decision for the good of this family.”

I laughed, but it came out broken. “You made a decision for your favorite. You didn’t sell his gaming setup. You didn’t sell anything you paid for. You sold the one thing that was mine.”

Nigeria is spending billions — not on weapons, but on persuasion.

And the target is the United States government.
The Federal Government has reportedly paid about $9 million (₦12.8 billion) to a U.S.-based lobbying firm to promote its narrative on the protection of Christians in Nigeria and counter growing international criticism.

According to documents filed with the U.S. Department of Justice, a Kaduna-based law firm, Aster Legal, engaged American lobbying and public affairs firm DCI Group on behalf of the Office of the National Security Adviser, headed by Nuhu Ribadu. 

The contract was signed on December 17, 2025, and will initially last six months, with an automatic renewal clause.

Under the deal, DCI Group is expected to help the Nigerian government communicate its efforts to safeguard Christian communities and maintain U.S. backing in the fight against jihadist groups and other security threats in West Africa.

Nigeria has already paid $4.5 million as an upfront retainer, with the agreement providing for a monthly payment of $750,000, split into two six-month installments.

This move comes amid intensified scrutiny from the U.S., following Nigeria’s redesignation as a “country of particular concern” over allegations of killings of Christians — claims Abuja has consistently rejected. The pressure increased further after U.S. airstrikes targeted terrorist enclaves in Sokoto during the Christmas period.
With insecurity still troubling citizens at home, many Nigerians are now asking: is lobbying abroad the right priority, or should those billions be spent fixing the problem on the ground?

Muhammad Ali was driving through rural Georgia in the sweltering summer of 1974 when he saw something that made his blood boil: a small diner with a crudely scrawled sign in the window that read “WHITES ONLY.” His heart pounded—not with fear, but with righteous anger. Around him, his entourage tried to reason with him. “Don’t go in, Muhammad. It’s not worth it,” his trainer Angelo Dundee warned. His friend and photographer Howard Bingham shook his head. Even Bundini Brown, his ever-loyal assistant, pleaded for caution. But Ali had other plans.

Ali’s life had already been a testament to courage and principle. Just three months earlier, he had shocked the world by defeating George Foreman in Kinshasa, Zaire, in the legendary Rumble in the Jungle, reclaiming the heavyweight championship of the world. He was at the peak of his fame and power, a man who could command arenas and headlines with a single word, yet what mattered to him most in that moment was justice and dignity. At 32 years old, Muhammad Ali was not just a champion in the ring—he was a champion for his people, for humanity itself.

The diner sat on a lonely country road, its windows reflecting the harsh sun and a society that had spent decades teaching some that Black lives were lesser. For Ali, the injustice was unbearable. He could not simply drive past. With calm authority, he parked his car, stepped out, and walked toward the entrance. His entourage followed silently, hearts pounding, knowing that whatever happened next could ignite anger, fear, or violence—but Ali walked as if he owned the moment, because in a moral sense, he did.

Inside, the owner looked up from wiping a counter, his expression a mixture of surprise and defensiveness. Ali spoke, his voice steady but commanding, carrying the weight of someone who had faced far greater battles than this. In the next ten minutes, he didn’t raise his fists. He didn’t curse or shout. He spoke with the power of reason, truth, and empathy. He reminded the man of something far greater than the rules of segregation: the dignity of human beings, the injustice of hatred, and the courage it takes to do the right thing.

By the time Ali left the diner, the owner was in tears, as were several of the patrons. What had been an ordinary summer afternoon in rural Georgia became a transformative moment. A man who had never questioned the norms of a segregated society suddenly confronted the humanity he had denied, and it shook him to his core. Ali, the poet of the ring, had delivered a knockout that no one would forget—one that transcended fists and belts and titles. It was a victory of the human spirit.

In that quiet, ordinary diner, Muhammad Ali reminded the world that true strength is not measured by how hard you hit, but by how boldly you stand for justice, how fiercely you protect dignity, and how unflinchingly you confront wrong, even when it is easier to look away. For Ali, every step, every word, every moment was an extension of his lifelong fight—not just against opponents in the ring, but against the deeply entrenched injustices of a nation still grappling with its conscience.

That summer day in 1974, Ali’s punch landed not on flesh but on the very soul of a man who had once thought he could deny others their humanity. And in that moment, the world glimpsed why Muhammad Ali was more than a champion—he was a force for moral courage, a man whose every action demanded reflection, accountability, and, above all, hope.

PÈRE DOMINIQUE ADEYEMI, MÉMOIRE VIVANTE DE LA VALLÉE DE L’OUÉMÉ

  Ce mercredi 14 janvier 2026,
l'église catholique *Sainte Thérèse de l’Enfant Jésus d’Azowlissè* accueille fidèles, clergé et populations de la vallée de l’Ouémé pour une messe d’action de grâce marquant le 45ᵉ anniversaire du décès du Patriarche Père Dominique ADEYEMI, 4ᵉ prêtre du Bénin.
Cette célébration, devenue un rendez-vous spirituel et mémoriel, ouvre chaque année le cycle de préparation de Wémèxwé, événement identitaire majeur pour la communauté.
Après le chapelet du matin, la messe sera suivie d’un recueillement sur la tombe du Père, geste fort de communion entre les générations et de fidélité à l’héritage spirituel laissé par cet homme d’Église d’exception.
 *Mais qui était réellement le Père Dominique ADEYEMI, ce prêtre dont le nom demeure profondément enraciné dans la mémoire collective de la vallée de l’Ouémé* ?
Un fils de Porto-Novo appelé très tôt au service de Dieu
Né en 1901 à Porto-Novo, Dominique ADEYEMI grandit à une époque charnière où le christianisme s’implante progressivement dans les territoires du Dahomey.
Il reçoit le baptême le 11 mai 1913 à la Cathédrale de Porto-Novo, marquant le début d’un cheminement spirituel profond et durable.
En avril 1920, animé par une foi ardente et une vocation déjà affirmée, il entre au séminaire de Ouidah, lieu historique de formation du clergé autochtone.
Un prêtre parmi les pionniers de l’Église au Bénin
Après plusieurs années de formation et de ministères préparatoires, Dominique ADEYEMI est ordonné diacre le 9 octobre 1932, puis prêtre le 30 octobre 1932 à la Basilique de Ouidah, par Mgr François STEINMETZ, lors de la solennité du Christ Roi de l’Univers.
Cette ordination le place parmi les premiers prêtres béninois, à une époque où l’Église locale comptait encore très peu de clercs autochtones. Il devient ainsi l’un des bâtisseurs silencieux de l’Église catholique au Bénin.
Azowlissè, terre de mission et de fidélité
Si le ministère du Père ADEYEMI l’a conduit successivement à Ouidah, Calavi et Djègbé, c’est à Azowlissè que son nom s’inscrit de manière indélébile dans l’histoire.
Le 19 mars 1937, il est au cœur de la création de la Paroisse Sainte Thérèse de l’Enfant Jésus d’Azowlissè, qu’il servira avec constance, humilité et dévouement pendant 33 années, jusqu’en 1970.
Pasteur proche de son peuple, éducateur de conscience, artisan de paix, il accompagne les familles, forme les jeunes, consolide la foi et participe à l’enracinement du christianisme dans les réalités culturelles locales.
Un ministère marqué par l’humilité et le don total
En 1970, le Père Dominique ADEYEMI est affecté à la Paroisse Notre-Dame de l’Atlantique de Djègbé, où il poursuit son service sacerdotal jusqu’en 1980.
Affaibli par l’âge, il se retire ensuite à l’Asile – Maison Saint Vincent de Paul des OCPSP à Calavi, lieu de repos et de prière, fidèle jusqu’au bout à son engagement sacerdotal.
Il s’éteint le 14 janvier 1981 à Calavi, laissant derrière lui une Église reconnaissante et un peuple profondément marqué.
Son inhumation a lieu le 22 janvier 1981 au cimetière paroissial d’Azowlissè, la terre même qu’il a tant aimée et servie.

 *Un héritage spirituel toujours vivant* 

Quarante-cinq ans après sa disparition, le Père Dominique ADEYEMI demeure une figure tutélaire pour la vallée de l’Ouémé.
Son nom est associé à la fidélité sacerdotale, à la *simplicité évangélique, au miracle, au dialogue entre foi et culture,* valeurs qui inspirent encore aujourd’hui les célébrations de Wémèxwé.
À travers la messe commémorative, le recueillement sur sa tombe et la transmission de sa mémoire, c’est toute une communauté qui affirme que les bâtisseurs de l’âme ne meurent jamais.
Père Dominique ADEYEMI,
serviteur de Dieu, patriarche de foi,
la vallée de l’Ouémé se souvient.

Good morning, Basse ❤️

Regarding the patrol our officers carried out and which we posted about yesterday,yes, we saw the reactions. While many people encouraged and applauded them for their efforts, others chose mockery and criticism. That is their right, but we sincerely believe our officers deserve better from us as a community.

Policing is not an easy job. It is risky, demanding, and often thankless. Some mocked their shoes, others questioned their motives,but very few stopped to think about the reality of their lives. Many of these officers earn very little. Some are not there for the money at all, but out of love for their country and a genuine desire to serve and protect their people. You would be shocked to hear their personal struggles: officers with four wives, many children in school, families to feed,yet they still wake up every day, put on that uniform, and stand between us and danger.

To those saying the police are witch~hunting motorcycle riders instead of mounting borders: let us be honest with ourselves. While not all motorcycle owners are involved, we all know that some criminals depend on motorcycles at night to carry out robberies and other crimes. Some riders knowingly or unknowingly assist them. Addressing this issue is not witch~hunting,it is crime prevention.

@WOB: Let us all play our part. Let us be our own police officers by reporting suspicious activities and standing for what is right. Crime in Basse has risen, and this is not the Basse we used to know. We now see young people,some barely out of childhood,venturing into smoking, rape, stealing, and other dangerous behaviors. If we remain silent, we become part of the problem.

From a reliable source, we’ve learned that motorcycle owners are organizing a meeting today to discuss important issues. This is a good step. Dialogue, cooperation, and mutual respect between the community and security forces are the way forward.

Let us choose to encourage our police officers, not discourage them. Let us uplift those who risk their lives daily for our safety. A community that respects and supports its protectors is a community that will thrive.

Basse can be peaceful again,if we all decide to be part of the solution 🤍

Today, I represented H.E. Hon. Rev. Mutuma M'Ethingia at the Development Partnership Forum (DPF), a high level engagement bringing together national leadership and development partners to deliberate on food and nutrition security, drought response, and climate resilience.

The forum reviewed progress made since the 13th DPF, including actions and commitments aimed at strengthening coordinated drought preparedness and response. Key presentations outlined the current food and nutrition security situation in the country and emphasized the role of early warning systems, timely interventions, and sustainable agricultural practices.

The discussions reinforced the importance of close collaboration between the national government, development partners, and county governments. For Meru County, these deliberations directly inform policies and programmes that protect livelihoods, enhance agricultural productivity, and support vulnerable communities, as we align our development agenda with national priorities to deliver tangible impact at the grassroots.

Good morning 🌞 

Peace in the Process* 

 *Today's Scripture* 

Yet you, LORD, are our Father. We are the clay, you are the potter; we are all the work of your hand. 
(Isaiah 64:8)

 *Today's Word* 

It’s easy to get down on ourselves because we’re not as far along as we want to be. We focus on our flaws, our shortcomings. Some people live with this nagging feeling inside, telling them something is wrong with them and how they don’t measure up. We put all this pressure on ourselves to change. “I have to be more disciplined…watch my mouth…be a better mother…a better spouse…a better employee.” But God is our potter. He controls the process of changing and shaping you, the when and how fast. The key is to accept yourself while God is in the process. 

You’re not supposed to go through life being against yourself, overanalyzing your faults. Focusing on your weaknesses doesn’t help you improve. All that does is slow down the process. It’s good to want to improve, to strive for excellence and grow, but don’t get frustrated if it’s not happening as fast as you’d like. Only God can change some things. Until He gives you the grace to do it, accept yourself where you are. It takes maturity to have peace in the process. 

 *Prayer For Today* 

Father, thank You that You are the potter and I am the clay. Thank You for where I am right now, faults and all, and that You hold me in Your hands and are molding and making me. Help me to stop being against myself and to simply trust You with the process for further change. In Jesus’ Name, Amen.

🕊️ Humanitarian Peace Statement
Launching the “Bridges of Peace” Initiative

We, the sons and daughters of peoples who have known pain, displacement, and the struggle for survival,
believe that the value of a human being is greater than any conflict, and that peace is not weakness, but courage and responsibility.

I, Alan Wali, a Kurd and humanitarian activist,
hereby announce the launch of the “Bridges of Peace” initiative
as a non-political, humanitarian civil initiative aimed at:

▪️ Promoting dialogue between Kurds and Israelis
▪️ Breaking down mutual stereotypes
▪️ Defending the lives of all civilians without exception
▪️ Transforming our shared memory of suffering into a force for understanding and peace

Kurds and Israelis are two peoples who have experienced persecution and attempts at erasure,
who have held firmly to their identity despite diaspora,
and who have believed that life is stronger than hatred.

Through this initiative, we affirm:
▪️ Our absolute rejection of targeting civilians
▪️ Our belief that peace begins with peoples, not with weapons
▪️ Our respect for the right of all peoples to live in safety and dignity

This initiative is neither political nor partisan.
It is a humanitarian call open to all who believe that the future is built through dialogue, not bloodshed.

From Kurdistan to Israel…
Humanity first, and peace is our path.

Alan Wali
Founder of the Bridges of Peace Initiative
Kurdish peace activist – Kurdish–Israeli rapprochement

COSE STRANE AL LUNA-PARK DI PORTA GALLIERA
( di Alberto Angelici)i

C’era una volta, al luna-park che ogni anno alzava le tende a porta Galliera dove oggi  sorge la stazione delle autocorriere, un uomo alto e un poco strano. 

Nell’ambiente si diceva che venisse da Como e che in gioventù avesse fatto la prigione a causa di una donna maritata.

Assieme alla moglie, Oscar, questo era il suo nome, gestiva un vecchio tendone tutto rappezzato dove, a mo’ di spettacolo, sfilavano bizzarre creature: la donna barbuta, una capra con due teste, un orso albino, un dromedario in età di pensione, un nano dai piedi giganteschi, un uomo enorme che rompeva una catena gonfiando il torace, ecc, ecc. 

In piedi su di una cassetta da frutta, l’uomo riscuoteva dando i biglietti, ripetendo in continuo e con voce stentorea sempre la stessa frase, mentre il grosso pomo d’Adamo andava su e giù come una molla:

“venghino, venghino, siori, venghino, che più gente entra e più bestie si veddono!”

Perché più persone entravano più lui incassava e più lui incassava e più creature strane poteva “arruolare” e mostrare al suo pubblico.

Fu così che nacque il detto che di certo molti di voi hanno già sentito ma senza conoscerne l’origine.

In uno spazio circolare non tanto grande, illuminato da due lampade teatrali sfilavano i personaggi, annunciati, uno dopo l’altro, da Oscar che, finito di incassare e chiuso l’ingresso velocemente sostituiva la giacchetta grigia con una marsina di paillettes assumendo il ruolo di presentatore per annunciare, uno dopo l’altro, l’entrata in scena dei protagonisti dello spettacolo. 

Soltanto l’orso, comperato cucciolo da un guardiacaccia abruzzese, avanzava al guinzaglio dell’uomo spezza-catene. 

Anche la moglie aveva più di un compito, multi-tasking si direbbe oggi. A lei spettava l’onere di allineare gli sgabelli riservati gli spettatori, ripulire dopo ogni spettacolo gettando un pugno di sabbia sugli eventuali scaracci e anche girare fra il pubblico con una piccola cassetta  bianca appesa al collo vendendo brustulli, caramelle gommose e aranciate San Pellegrino.

Il tendone delle strane creature, così era chiamato, chiuse all’improvviso i battenti in una tiepida domenica di fine maggio quando Oscar, travolto da un’insana, sorprendente passione, fuggì assieme all’uomo spezza-catene e di loro nulla più si seppe.

VIC Bushfire Watch & Act: East Natimuk, Grass Flat, Natimuk, Quantong, Vectis
 
Details:
 
VIC Bushfire Watch & Act: East Natimuk, Grass Flat, Natimuk, Quantong, Vectis
 
This Watch and Act message is being issued for East Natimuk, Grass Flat, Natimuk, Quantong, Vectis, Vectis East, Vectis South.

The grass fire in the Natimuk, Quantong and Vectis area is now under control.
  
Firefighters, emergency services and Powercor continue to work on the fire, treatment of fire affected trees and repair of powerlines. 

Residents should stay alert for dangerous trees and electrical hazards. 

The Wimmera Highway is open for all traffic through Natimuk. 

All roads east of the Wimmera River in the Quantong and Vectis area are open.

Some local roads remain closed due to safety concerns. Continue to obey all road signage. 
A recovery centre is open from 10am to 4pm daily at 62 Main Street, Natimuk.

For recovery information visit the HRCC Grass Flat Fire Recovery web page. 

This Watch and Act message replaces the Watch and Act issued at 4:49pm Tuesday 13th January 2026.

While the threat is reduced, there is still active fire in the area. The situation can change at any time. You must monitor conditions and be ready to act.
What you should do:  
Monitor changes to the situation, noting there is still active fire in the area. Increased wind speed, change in wind direction, smoke or poor visibility can indicate the situation is changing.  
Watch out for essential services and vehicles working in the area.

Leave if the situation changes or you feel unsafe. Remember to: 
Take your pets, medications, mobile phone and charger. 
Many people have died trying to leave at the last minute during fires. 
Travel to the home of family or friends that are away from the warning area. 
Impacts in your area: 
There are still powerlines down and hazardous trees within the fire ground.
Access to Natimuk township is via the Wimmera Highway only.
Check the VicTraffic website (traffic.transport.vic.gov.au) or call 13 11 70 for road closures.
This message was issued by Country Fire Authority.   

The next update is expected by 15/01/2026 05:20 pm or as the situation changes.   

Use multiple sources to stay informed: 
www.emergency.vic.gov.au 
VicEmergency Hotline - freecall 1800 226 226 
VicEmergency app 
Facebook or Twitter (#VicFires) 
ABC Local Radio, commercial and designated community radio stations, or Sky News TV.   
Accessibility: 
The following services can help you, or someone you know, access information during an emergency. For information in other languages, call the Translating and Interpreting Service on 131 450 (freecall) and ask them to call VicEmergency Hotline.   
If you are deaf, hard of hearing, or have a speech/communication impairment contact National Relay Service for assistance and ask them to call the VicEmergency Hotline.
 
Alert Source: VIC Emergency
 
Early Warning Network: https://www.earlywarningnetwork.com.au
Council Resident Options & EWN Support: https://www.earlywarningnetwork.com.au/support

Midpoint Nigeria Pays Courtesy Visit to Jos North LGA Chairman in Furtherance of FORB Need Assessment Project

As part of its ongoing stakeholder engagement for the Freedom of Religion or Belief (FORB) Need Assessment Project, Midpoint Nigeria paid a courtesy visit to the Chairman of Jos North Local Government Area, Barr. J. K. Chris.

The visit was aimed at formally introducing Midpoint Nigeria, sharing the vision and objectives of the FORB Need Assessment Project, and seeking the support and collaboration of the Jos North Local Government toward the successful implementation of the project.

The Midpoint Nigeria delegation was led by Rev. Joshua Sati, Director of Faith and Community Development, alongside Mr. Joshua Chohu, Director of Finance and Administration. 

During the engagement, the delegation explained that the FORB Need Assessment Project is designed to identify key challenges, gaps, and opportunities relating to freedom of religion or belief within communities in Jos North. 

The project adopts a community-based and evidence-driven approach, with a strong focus on strengthening peaceful coexistence, social cohesion, and providing data to inform responsive governance and peacebuilding interventions.

In his response, Barr. J. K. Chris warmly received the team and commended Midpoint Nigeria for its dedication to peacebuilding and community development. He acknowledged the relevance of the FORB Need Assessment Project, particularly in a religiously diverse local government area like Jos North, and expressed his openness to initiatives that promote mutual understanding, tolerance, and sustainable peace.

The meeting concluded with a shared understanding of the critical role that collaboration between local government authorities and civil society organizations plays in addressing issues related to freedom of religion or belief. 

The Chairman further encouraged continuous engagement with relevant departments and community stakeholders to ensure the effectiveness and success of the project.

Midpoint Nigeria sincerely appreciates Barr. J. K. Chris for his time, hospitality, and willingness to support initiatives that foster peace, inclusion, and harmonious coexistence in Jos North Local Government Area.

#MidpointNigeria #FORB #Peacebuilding #StakeholderEngagement #FreedomOfReligionOrBelief #SocialCohesion #JosNorth #CommunityDevelopment

What is writing process? Writing process is a step-by-step method writers use to develop a clear and effective test.

WRITING is a fundamental form of human expression that enables individuals to convey their thoughts, ideas, emotions, and experiences through the use of written symbols, such as letters, words, and sentences. It is a vital tool for communication, allowing people to share their perspectives, insights, and knowledge with others across time and space.

REVISION is like giving your writing a makeover. It's where you take a closer look at what you've written, tweak it, and make it shine. You smooth out rough edges, fix awkward sentences, and make sure your message comes across loud and clear

EDITING simply means "FIXING", such as misspelling, grammar, punctuation and capitalization. To avoid confusion from the readers, example is when you're talking about "PEACE" but you misspelled it as "PIECE", you're gonna confuse the readers and misunderstand your point.

PUBLISHING is the final stage, where you can finally share your work with your intended audience, transforming ideas and effort into something accessible, visible, and impactful. It marks the transition from creation to communication, allowing your message to reach readers, spark engagement, and contribute meaningfully to your field or community.

" ISSUES AND CONCERNS " NG IP COMMUNITY SA LALAWIGAN NG MAGUINDANAO DEL SUR NA MAY KAUGNAYAN SA " LUPA ", TINALAKAY AT GINAWAAN NG MABISANG SOLUSYON SA PAMAMAGITAN NG PEACE DIALOGUE AT OPEN FORUM NA DINALUHAN NG IBAT IBANG STAKEHOLDERS SA PANGUNGUNA NI GOVERNOR KAGUI ALI MIDTIMBANG, MILITAR, PNP, MIPA, LGU OFFICIALS, IP LEADERS AT IBA PA

Tinalakay sa isinagawang Peace Dialogue and Open Forum ang mga hamon at suliranin na kinakaharap nang mga Indigenous People ( IP )  sa bayan ng Datu Hoffer, sa pangunguna ng 601st Infantry Brigade PA sa pamumuno ni BGEN. Edgar Catu at Maguindanao del Sur Governor Datu Ali Midtimbang, na ginanap sa Training Center, Datu Hoffer Municipal Compound, Barangay Limpongo, Datu Hoffer, Maguindanao del Sur, ngayong araw ng Miyerkules, January 14, 2026.

Ang naturang Peace Dialogue and Open Forum ay nilahukan ng mga Line Agencies, Community Leaders, Stakeholders, Business Sectors, PNP, AFP, MIPA BARMM, MPOS BARMM, MENRE BARMM, MILF-CCCH, LGU Shariff Aguak, LGU Datu Hoffer at iba pa.

Tinalakay sa pagpupulong ang IP ancestral domain, IPRA law, at proteksyon ng mga katutubong pamayanan sa bayan ng Datu Hoffer, Maguindanao del Sur.

Layunin ng nasabing peace dialogue na maiwasan ang mga hindi kanais-nais na mga pangyayari na madalas biktima ang maliliit at inosenteng mamamayan.

Sa naging mensahe ni Maguindanao del Sur Governor Datu Ali Midtimbang, kanyang sinabi na sa pakikipagtulungan ng lokal na pamahalaan ng Shariff Aguak at ng LGU Datu Hoffer ay tutulungan ang mga apektadong mga IP na magawaan ng titulo ang mga lupang kanilang sinasaka upang tuluyan na itong maisaayos at makapamuhay ng mapayapa.

Binigyang-diin ni Gobernador Midtimbang na sa pamamagitan ng maayos na pag-uusap ay walang hindi kayang mabigyan ng solusyon.

|| Jasper Acosta

LUGUS, Sulu — 14 January 2026 — The 104th Infantry “Para sa Bayan” Battalion, under the leadership of LTC ROY R DALUMPINES INF (GSC) PA, Commanding Officer, successfully conducted a community engagement activity at Bas Lugus, Municipality of Lugus, Sulu, focusing on people’s organization and the empowerment of local fisherfolk.

The activity was spearheaded on the ground by 1LT ROYLANDO C GARCIA (INF) PA, Civil-Military Operations (CMO) Officer of 104IB, together with 2LT JUNER T GUIMBARDA (INF) PA, Platoon Leader of Bravo Company, and SSG Naddin, Chief CMO NCO. The team conducted a pulong-pulong (community dialogue) aimed at fostering unity, cooperation, and sustainable livelihood opportunities among fisherfolk members in the area.

The community dialogue was led by Alnajib B. Maujon, Imam of Masjid Abdussamad, whose leadership played a vital role in encouraging active community participation and collaboration. Members of the local fisherfolk community actively engaged in discussions centered on people’s organization, lawful livelihood initiatives, and collective efforts toward peace and development.

The activity emphasized the importance of strengthening partnerships between the community and government institutions to promote peace, stability, and socio-economic progress. Participants expressed their appreciation for the initiative and conveyed their willingness to support and take part in future community development programs.

The pulong-pulong was conducted in a peaceful and orderly manner, reflecting the community’s growing commitment to unity and cooperation. This initiative underscores the continued dedication of the 104th Infantry “Para sa Bayan” Battalion in supporting grassroots development and sustaining peace through inclusive and people-centered engagements.

High Blood Pressure 

High blood pressure is a condition where the force of blood against the blood vessels is too high. Over time, this pressure damages the body silently.

Many people live with high blood pressure without knowing, because it may not show symptoms early.

What causes high blood pressure?
• Too much salt and oily food
• Stress and lack of rest
• Obesity and lack of exercise
• Smoking and alcohol
• Family history
• Poor blood circulation

These are the possible warning signs when it gets worse, Headaches, Dizziness, Chest discomfort, Fast heartbeat, Fatigue or blurry vision.

Why should you take it seriously?  Did you that that uncontrolled high blood pressure can lead to Stroke, heart attack, heart failure, kidney damage and lot more..

Remember: High blood pressure is not something to ignore. Early care saves lives!

Bantahan Telak Bagi Pelaku Bid’ah

Ketika ahlul bidah dinasehati agar jangan melakukan bid’ah, mereka akan mengatakan “ini kan baik, kenapa anda melarang orang berbuat baik?”

Mengapa Pelaku Bid’ah Sulit Bertaubat?

Orang-orang yang terjerumus dalam kebid’ahan, mereka menyangka kebid’ahan yang ada pada mereka itu sebagai kebaikan. Sehingga sulit bagi mereka untuk bertaubat dari kebid’ahan tersebut. Dan ketika diingkari oleh Ahlussunnah, mereka malah menuduh Ahlussunnah mengingkari kebaikan.

Misalnya, orang-orang yang melakukan dzikir-dzikir bid’ah, ketika dinasehati mereka malah mengatakan, “mengapa kalian mengingkari dzikir? Apakah kalian benci dzikir?”.

Orang-orang yang membaca shalawat-shalawat bid’ah, ketika dinasehati mereka mengatakan, “mengapa kalian mengingkari shalawat? Apakah kalian benci shalawat?”. Dan seterusnya.

Nasihat Ulama Tentang Pelaku Bid’ah

Perhatikan nasehat Sa’id bin Musayyab rahimahullah berikut ini.

Sa’id bin Musayyab adalah seorang ulama besar di kalangan tabi’in, yang beliau dijuluki “alim ahlil Madinah” (ulamanya penduduk Madinah) dan juga “sayyidut tabi’in” (pemimpinnya para tabi’in). Diriwayatkan oleh Al Baihaqi dalam Sunan-nya,

رأى سعيد بن المسيب رجلا يصلي بعد طلوع الفجر أكثر من ركعتين يكثر فيها الركوع والسجود فنهاه. فقال: يا أبا محمد! أيعذبني الله على الصلاة؟! قال: لا ولكن يعذبك على خلاف السنة

“Sa’id bin al Musayyab melihat seorang yang shalat setelah terbit fajar lebih dari dua raka’at, yang ia memperpanjang rukuk dan sujudnya. Lalu Sa’id bin al Musayyab melarangnya. 

Maka orang tadi berkata: Wahai Abu Muhammad, apakah Allah akan mengazab saya gara-gara saya shalat?

Sa’id bin al Musayyab menjawab: bukan demikian, namun Allah akan mengazabmu karena menyelisihi sunnah” (Diriwayatkan Al Baihaqi dalam Sunan Al Kubra, 2/466, Ad Darimi 1/404-405, dishahihkan Al Albani dalam Irwa’ul Ghalil, 2/236).

Ketika ahlul bidah dinasehati agar jangan melakukan bid’ah, mereka akan mengatakan “ini kan baik, kenapa anda melarang orang berbuat baik?”. Maka jawaban Sa’id bin Musayyab rahimahullah adalah jawaban telak. Yang dilarang bukan ibadahnya, namun bagian dari ibadah tersebut, baik penetapannya, tata caranya, pengkhususan waktu atau tempatnya, jumlah bilangannya dan semisalnya yang tidak ada tuntunannya dari sunnah Rasulullah Shallallahu’alaihi Wasallam.

Syaikh Muhammad Nashiruddin Al Albani rahimahullah mengomentari riwayat ini dengan mengatakan:

وهذا من بدائع أجوبة سعيد بن المسيب – رحمه الة تعالى -, وهو سلاح قوي على المبتدعة الذين يستحسنون كثيرا من البدع باسم أنها ذكر وصلاة ثم ينكرون على أهل السنة إنكار ذلك عليهم ويتهمونهم بأنهم ينكرون الذكر والصلاة!! وهم في الحقيقة إنما ينكرون خلافهم للسنة في الذكر والصلاة ونحو ذلك

“Ini merupakan diantara jawaban yang sangat telak dari Sa’id bin al Musayyab. Dan ini juga merupakan senjata bagi para ahlul bid’ah yang mereka menganggap baik banyak sekali perbuatan bid’ah, dengan mengatakan bahwa yang mereka lakukan itu dzikir dan shalat. 

Kemudian mereka malah mengingkari Ahlussunnah yang mengingkari bid’ah mereka dengan mengesankan bahwa Ahlussunnah mengingkari dzikir dan shalat! 

Padahal yang diingkari oleh Ahlussunnah adalah penentangan mereka terhadap sunnah dalam dzikir dan shalat serta ibadah lainnya” (Irwa’ul Ghalil, 2/236).

Para Sahabat Nabi Mengingkari Amalan Bid’ah

Demikian pula para sahabat Nabi ridhwanullah ‘alaihim, mereka mengingkari orang yang melakukan ibadah jika disertai kebid’ahan. Walaupun niatnya baik dan bentuknya adalah ibadah. Sebagaimana Abdullah bin Mas’ud radhiallahu’anhu, beliau mengingkari orang-orang yang berdzikir secara berjama’ah di masjid. Dikisahkan oleh Abu Musa Al Asy’ari radhiallahu’anhu:

قال رأيتُ في المسجدِ قومًا حِلَقًا جلوسًا ينتظرون الصلاةَ في كلِّ حلْقةٍ رجلٌ وفي أيديهم حصًى فيقول كَبِّرُوا مئةً فيُكبِّرونَ مئةً فيقول هلِّلُوا مئةً فيُهلِّلون مئةً ويقول سبِّحوا مئةً فيُسبِّحون مئةً قال فماذا قلتَ لهم قال ما قلتُ لهم شيئًا انتظارَ رأيِك قال أفلا أمرتَهم أن يعُدُّوا سيئاتِهم وضمنتَ لهم أن لا يضيعَ من حسناتهم شيءٌ ثم مضى ومضَينا معه حتى أتى حلقةً من تلك الحلقِ فوقف عليهم فقال ما هذا الذي أراكم تصنعون قالوا يا أبا عبدَ الرَّحمنِ حصًى نعُدُّ به التكبيرَ والتهليلَ والتَّسبيحَ قال فعُدُّوا سيئاتِكم فأنا ضامنٌ أن لا يضيعَ من حسناتكم شيءٌ ويحكم يا أمَّةَ محمدٍ ما أسرعَ هلَكَتِكم هؤلاءِ صحابةُ نبيِّكم صلَّى اللهُ عليهِ وسلَّمَ مُتوافرون وهذه ثيابُه لم تَبلَ وآنيتُه لم تُكسَرْ والذي نفسي بيده إنكم لعلى مِلَّةٍ هي أهدى من ملةِ محمدٍ أو مُفتتِحو بابَ ضلالةٍ قالوا والله يا أبا عبدَ الرَّحمنِ ما أردْنا إلا الخيرَ قال وكم من مُريدٍ للخيرِ لن يُصيبَه إنَّ رسولَ اللهِ صلَّى اللهُ عليهِ وسلَّمَ حدَّثنا أنَّ قومًا يقرؤون القرآنَ لا يجاوزُ تراقيهم يمرُقونَ من الإسلامِ كما يمرُقُ السَّهمُ منَ الرَّميّةِ وأيمُ اللهِ ما أدري لعلَّ أكثرَهم منكم ثم تولى عنهم فقال عمرو بنُ سلَمةَ فرأينا عامَّةَ أولئك الحِلَقِ يُطاعِنونا يومَ النَّهروانِ مع الخوارجِ

“Abu Musa Al Asy’ari berkata: aku melihat di masjid ada beberapa orang yang duduk membuat halaqah sambil menunggu shalat. Setiap halaqah ada seorang (pemimpin) yang memegangi kerikil, kemudian ia berkata: bertakbirlah 100 kali! Maka para pesertanya pun bertakbir 100 kali. Kemudian pemimpinnya berkata: bertahlil lah 100 kali! Maka para pesertanya pun bertahlil 100 kali. Kemudian pemimpinnya berkata: bertasbih lah 100 kali! Maka para pesertanya pun bertasbih 100 kali. 

Ibnu Mas’ud berkata: lalu apa yang engkau katakan kepada mereka wahai Abu Musa? Abu Musa menjawab: aku tidak katakan apapun karena menunggu pandanganmu. Ibnu Mas’ud berkata: mengapa tidak engkau katakan saja pada mereka: hitunglah keburukan-keburukan kalian saja, maka aku jamin kebaikan-kebaikan kalian tidak akan disia-siakan sama sekali. 

Kemudian Ibnu Mas’ud pergi dan kami pun pergi bersama beliau. Sampai pada suatu hari Ibnu Mas’ud mendapati sendiri halaqah tersebut. Lalu beliau pun berdiri di hadapan mereka.

Ibnu Mas’ud berkata: apa yang kalian lakukan ini? Mereka menjawab: Wahai Abu Abdirrahman, ini adalah kerikil untuk menghitung takbir, tahlil dan tasbih! Ibnu Mas’ud berkata: hitunglah keburukan-keburukan kalian saja, maka aku jamin kebaikan-kebaikan kalian tidak akan disia-siakan sama sekali. Wahai umat Muhammad, betapa cepatnya kalian binasa! Demi Allah, yang kalian lakukan ini adalah ajaran agama yang lebih baik dari ajaran Muhammad atau kalian sedang membuka pintu kesesatan!

Mereka mengatakan: Wahai Abu Abdirrahman, kami tidak menginginkan apa-apa kecuali kebaikan! Ibnu Mas’ud menjawab: betapa banyak orang yang menginginkan kebaikan namun tidak mendapatkannya. Sesungguhnya Rasulullah mengatakan kepada kami tentang suatu kaum yang mereka membaca Al-Qur’an akan tetapi (bacaan mereka) tidak melewati tenggorokan mereka, demi Allah, saya tidak tahu bisa jadi kebanyakan mereka adalah dari kalian. Kemudian Ibnu Mas’ud meninggalkan mereka”. 

Amr bin Salamah berkata , ”Kami melihat kebanyakan orang-orang yang ada di halaqah itu adalah orang-orang yang ikut melawan kami di barisan khawarij pada perang Nahrawan” (Diriwayatkan Ad Darimi dalam Sunan-nya no.210, dishahihkan Al Albani dalam As Silsilah Ash Shahihah, 5/11).

Lihatlah! Abdullah bin Mas’ud radhiallahu’anhu mengingkari orang-orang yang berdzikir, namun dzikir mereka dengan tata cara yang bid’ah. Apakah kita akan menuduh Ibnu Mas’ud melarang orang berdzikir?! Tentu tidak, karena yang beliau ingkari bukan ibadah dzikir namun dzikir yang disertai kebid’ahan.

Maka kebid’ahan tetaplah buruk walaupun dinamakan sebagai ibadah atau dzikir atau shalawat atau doa atau sebutan-sebutan baik lainnya. Abdullah bin Umar radhiallahu’anhuma mengatakan:

كلُّ بدعةِ ضلالةٍ وإن رآها النَّاسُ حَسنةً

“Setiap kebid’ahan itu sesat walaupun manusia menganggapnya baik” (Diriwayatkan Ibnu Bathah dalam Al Ibanah no. 175, Al Lalika-i dalam Syarah Ushul I’tiqad Ahlissunnah no. 104, dishahihkan Al Albani dalam Ishlahul Masajid hal. 13).

Semoga Allah memberi taufik.

Penulis: Yulian Purnama

身為PM的你
你以為自己是三軍統帥，
結果到頭來發現你只是個 NPC ...
身為盡責的NPC(?)，
就要學會專案管理相關的英文關鍵字
󠀠
📦 resource allocation [rɪˋsɔrs ͵æləˋkeʃən] 資源分配
定義：
將有限的資源（人力、時間、預算、設備等）分配給不同任務或團隊成員
白話文：披薩就那麼大塊，每個人都想要最大的那塊
󠀠
實用例句：
The project team argued over resource allocation… and ended up sharing only the office snacks.
（專案團隊為資源分配吵了一場……最後只分了辦公室零食。）
󠀠
延伸詞彙：
． resource management (n.) = 資源管理
． workload distribution (n.) = 工作量分配
． capacity planning (n.) = 產能規劃
． prioritization (n.) = 優先順序排定
． overallocation (n.) = 過度分配
． resource pool (n.) = 資源池
． trade-offs (n.) = 取捨
󠀠
🔍 risk assessment [rɪsk əˋsɛsmənt] 風險評估
定義：
在專案開始前或進行中，識別、分析和評估可能發生的風險
白話文：預測所有可能讓專案爆炸的地雷
因為莫非定律告訴我們 "Anything that can go wrong, will go wrong."
（會出錯的事情一定會出錯）
󠀠
實用例句：
Before starting the project, the manager insisted on a risk assessment, which mainly involved checking who was on vacation.
（在啟動專案前，經理堅持做風險評估，結果主要是看誰休假中。）
󠀠
延伸詞彙：
． risk mitigation (n.) = 風險緩解
． contingency plan (n.) = 應急計畫
． worst-case scenario (n.) = 最壞情況
． safety net (n.) = 安全網
． risk tolerance (n.) = 風險容忍度
． preventive measures (n.) = 預防措施
． early warning signs (n.) = 預警信號
󠀠
🍾 bottleneck [ˋbɑt!͵nɛk] 瓶頸、僵局
定義：
專案或流程中限制整體進度的環節
就像瓶子的窄頸部分，不管瓶身多大，液體流出的速度取決於瓶頸
(就看誰是瓶頸囉…)
󠀠
實用例句：
The new software solved many problems… except for the real bottleneck: approving documents.
（新軟體解決了很多問題……除了真正的瓶頸：文件審核。）
󠀠
延伸詞彙：
． roadblock (n.) = 障礙、阻礙
． constraint (n.) = 限制、約束
． throughput (n.) = 產出量、吞吐量
． workflow (n.) = 工作流程
． dependency (n.) = 依賴性
． single point of failure (n.) = 單點故障
． critical path (n.) = 關鍵路徑
󠀠
專案管理是一門藝術（也是一場災難管理）
They fail because of bad execution.
But mostly, they fail because of unrealistic expectations."
（專案失敗不是因為計畫不好
而是因為執行不好
但最主要是因為不切實際的期待）
所以，降低期待值
可能是最好的風險管理策略 😌✨
加油，專案經理們！You've got this! 💪
󠀠

January 14. Daily Inspiration Message:

Is Anger Justified?

God is Love and All. Therefore everything that is real, in truth, has Love as its foundation. This foundation may or may not appear to be visible or available in every moment, but it is always there. In every person, situation and event, this loving foundation and choice exists. The ego, placing itself in opposition to All, in truth, is nothing. Thus the ego’s tools, such as anger and judgment, are not a real expression of who we are. It is only when we believe that illusions are more real than truth that we get affected by them. In truth, anger is not real. Yet anger does indeed appear real to those who have yet to awaken to the truth in them. Do not despair. For even beyond the false concept of anger, the truth exists and patiently awaits your awakening.

Today, if angry thoughts arise, stop and recall where such thoughts take you — nowhere that you want to be. Now, in control of your thinking, use the angry thoughts, not to punish yourself or another, but instead to trigger you to choose a more productive path. Your true destination is the state of peace. If you would like to reach your destination, then you must proceed in its direction. Peace is an expression of Love, and thus any of Love’s expressions move you in the direction of peace. Forgiveness and gratitude are two such expressions. Forgive instead of being judgmental and angry, and you will find yourself on the path to peace. Then offer your gratitude, not your condemnation, to all those who are part of your healing and awakening process. Do so and your path to peace will be assured.

************

Thank you for liking and sharing.

Peace, Health, Happiness, Love, Laughter, and Light.

James Blanchard Cisneros
Author of the Book: You Have Chosen to Remember

Spot early warning signs of boiler trouble before the cold hits

As the chilly months approach, now's the perfect time to check your boiler's health — before it lets you down when you need it most.

Here are some early signs to watch out for:

• Strange noises like banging or clunking
• Boiler struggling to heat up or losing pressure
• Yellow rather than blue flame (could signal a safety issue)
• Radiators not heating evenly or at all
• Increased energy bills without extra usage

Catching these early means you can avoid costly breakdowns and emergency call-outs during winter's worst.

အခြေအနေတွေဟာ Sectarian Trap (ဂိုဏ်းဂဏစွဲ/လူမျိုးရေးအစွဲ ထောင်ချောက်) ရဲ့ ပြဋ္ဌာန်းစာအုပ်လာ နမူနာပုံစံတွေပဲ ဖြစ်ပါတယ်။

ဒီအခြေအနေကို ဘယ်လိုရှောင်လွှဲရမလဲ

(Discuss with Google AI Studio)

++++++++++ ++++++++++ ++++++++++

၁။ အခြေအနေကို ခွဲခြမ်းစိတ်ဖြာခြင်း (Context Analysis)
ရွေးကောက်ပွဲနီးချိန်မှာ ခုလိုဖြစ်ရပ်တွေ တပြိုင်နက်တည်း ဖြစ်လာတာဟာ တိုက်ဆိုင်မှု မဟုတ်ဘဲ "ဖန်တီးထားတဲ့ မုန်တိုင်း" (Calculated Chaos) ဖြစ်နိုင်ခြေ အလွန်များပါတယ်။

နယ်စပ်ပဋိပက္ခ (ထိုင်း-ကမ္ဘောဒီးယား): 
ဒါဟာ အမျိုးသားရေးဝါဒ (Nationalism) ကို နှိုးဆွပြီး ပြည်သူတွေရဲ့ အာရုံကို ပြည်တွင်းရေးကနေ ပြည်ပရန်ဘက်ဆီ လွှဲပြောင်းလိုက်တာပါ။

တောင်ပိုင်းဗုံးပေါက်ကွဲမှု: 
ဒါဟာ လုံခြုံရေးအရ စိုးရိမ်စိတ်ကို မြှင့်တင်ပြီး "တင်းကျပ်တဲ့ အုပ်ချုပ်မှု" ဒါမှမဟုတ် "လုံခြုံရေးကို အလေးပေးတဲ့ ခေါင်းဆောင်" ကို လိုလားလာအောင် ဖန်တီးတာပါ။

ဗမာ-ထိုင်း လူမျိုးရေးလှုံ့ဆော်မှု: 
ဒါဟာ အလွန်အန္တရာယ်များတဲ့ ထောင်ချောက်ပါ။ ရွှေ့ပြောင်းလုပ်သားတွေကို "ရန်သူ" အဖြစ် ပုံဖော်ခြင်းဖြင့် ပြည်တွင်းက လူငယ်တွေရဲ့ မကျေနပ်မှုတွေကို လမ်းကြောင်းလွှဲတာ ဖြစ်ပါတယ်။ ဇန်နဝါရီ ၅ ရက်က ဖြစ်ရပ်ကို ၁၃ ရက် ရက်စွဲနဲ့ ပြန်တင်တာဟာ "စနစ်တကျ လုပ်ဆောင်တဲ့ ဝါဒဖြန့်မှု" (Information Operation - IO) ဖြစ်ကြောင်း သက်သေပြနေပါတယ်။

...

၂။ ဘယ်လို ရှောင်လွှဲရမလဲ? (How to Avoid)

အဆင့် (၁) - သတင်းကို ဆိုင်းငံ့ကြည့်ရှုပါ (Pause before Reacting):
လူမျိုးရေး လှုံ့ဆော်မှုတွေ မြင်တဲ့အခါ ဒေါသ မထွက်ပါနဲ့ဦး။ "ဒါဟာ ငါ့ကို ဒေါသထွက်အောင် တမင်လုပ်တာလား?" လို့ ကိုယ့်ကိုယ်ကိုယ် မေးပါ။ ဒေါသဟာ စဉ်းစားဆင်ခြင်ဉာဏ်ကို ပိတ်ပစ်တတ်ပါတယ်။

အဆင့် (၂) - ရက်စွဲနဲ့ အချက်အလက်ကို စစ်ဆေးပါ (Verification):
ဇန်နဝါရီ ၅ ရက်က ဖြစ်ရပ်ကို ၁၃ ရက်လို့ ပြောင်းတင်တာမျိုးဟာ ထောင်ချောက်ရဲ့ သိသာတဲ့ အထောက်အထားပါ။ အဟောင်းတွေကို အသစ်လုပ်ပြီး တင်နေပြီဆိုရင် ဒါဟာ တမင် ရန်တိုက်ပေးနေတာလို့ တပ်အပ်သေချာ သိရှိလိုက်ပါ။

အဆင့် (၃) - အထွေထွေ ယေဘုယျပြုခြင်းကို ရှောင်ပါ (Avoid Generalization):
လူငယ်ချင်း ရန်ဖြစ်တာဟာ လူမျိုးနှစ်မျိုးကြား စစ်ပွဲမဟုတ်ပါဘူး။ "လူငယ်ချင်း ပြဿနာဖြစ်တာ" ကို "ဗမာနဲ့ ထိုင်း ချနေတာ" လို့ ပြောလာရင် အဲဒီစကားလုံးကို လက်မခံပါနဲ့။ တစ်ဦးချင်းစီရဲ့ လုပ်ရပ်ကို လူမျိုးစုတစ်ခုလုံးနဲ့ မရောထွေးပါနဲ့။

...

၃။ ဘယ်လို ချေဖျက်ရမလဲ? (How to Neutralize)

အဆင့် (၁) - သတင်းတုကို အမှန်ဖြင့် တုံ့ပြန်ပါ (Counter-Messaging):
Social Media ပေါ်မှာ ရက်စွဲအမှားနဲ့ တက်လာတဲ့ ဗီဒီယိုတွေအောက်မှာ "ဒါဟာ ဇန်နဝါရီ ၅ ရက်က ဖြစ်ရပ်ဟောင်းပါ၊ အခုဖြစ်တာမဟုတ်ပါဘူး" ဆိုတဲ့ အချက်အလက်အမှန် (Link/Screenshot နှင့်တကွ) ကို ယဉ်ကျေးစွာ Comment ရေးပါ။ အမုန်းစကားကို အချက်အလက်နဲ့ တိုက်ဖျက်ပါ။

အဆင့် (၂) - လှုံ့ဆော်သူတွေကို Report ထုပါ:
လူမျိုးရေး ခွဲခြားမှုနဲ့ အကြမ်းဖက်မှုကို အားပေးတဲ့ Post တွေကို တစ်ဦးချင်းရော၊ အုပ်စုလိုက်ပါ Report လုပ်ပြီး အဲဒီအကြောင်းအရာတွေ ပျံ့နှံ့မှုကို တားဆီးပါ။

အဆင့် (၃) - Sharing ကို ရပ်တန့်ပါ:

"ဒါကိုကြည့်ပြီး သတိထားကြပါ" ဆိုတဲ့ ရည်ရွယ်ချက်နဲ့ဖြစ်စေ အမုန်းစကားတွေကို Share တာဟာ အဲဒီအမုန်းတရား ပိုပျံ့အောင် ကူညီပေးရာ ရောက်ပါတယ်။ လုံးဝ Share မလုပ်ပါနဲ့။

...

၄။ ငြိမ်းချမ်းရေးကို ဘယ်လို တည်ဆောက်မလဲ? (Peace Building)

အဆင့် (၁) - လူမှုကွန်ရက် မဟာမိတ်ဖွဲ့ပါ (Digital Solidarity):
ထိုင်းနိုင်ငံမှာရှိတဲ့ မြန်မာအဖွဲ့အစည်းတွေနဲ့ ထိုင်းလူမှုရေးအဖွဲ့အစည်းတွေ ပူးပေါင်းပြီး "ငါတို့ဟာ ရန်သူတွေ မဟုတ်ဘူး၊ အတူတူ နေထိုင်ကြသူတွေပဲ" ဆိုတဲ့ Campaign မျိုးတွေ (ဥပမာ- ထိုင်းနဲ့ ဗမာလူငယ်တွေ အတူတူ အလုပ်လုပ်နေတာ၊ စားသောက်နေတာ) ကို တန်ပြန် ဖော်ပြပေးပါ။

အဆင့် (၂) - ဒေသခံ ဥပဒေစိုးမိုးရေးကို ယုံကြည်မှု တည်ဆောက်ပါ:
ပြဿနာဖြစ်လာရင် ကိုယ်တိုင် လက်စားမချေဘဲ သက်ဆိုင်ရာ ရဲစခန်း ဒါမှမဟုတ် အလုပ်သမား အဖွဲ့အစည်းတွေကို အကြောင်းကြားပါ။ ဥပဒေကြောင်းအရ ဖြေရှင်းခြင်းကသာ ပဋိပက္ခကို လူမျိုးရေးအသွင် မကူးစက်အောင် တားဆီးနိုင်မှာပါ။

အဆင့် (၃) - အသိပညာပေးခြင်း (Education):
ကိုယ့်ပတ်ဝန်းကျင်က မြန်မာလုပ်သားတွေကို "အခုချိန်ဟာ ရွေးကောက်ပွဲနီးလို့ အကဲဆတ်နေတဲ့ အချိန်ဖြစ်တယ်၊ ရန်ပွဲတွေ၊ လူစုလူဝေးတွေကို ရှောင်ပါ၊ သတင်းမှားတွေကို မယုံပါနဲ့" လို့ သတိပေး စကားပြောပေးပါ။

...

အနှစ်ချုပ် သုံးသပ်ချက်

လက်ရှိ ထိုင်းနိုင်ငံမှာ ဖြစ်နေတာဟာ "နိုင်ငံရေး အမြတ်ထုတ်လိုသူတွေက ပြည်သူတွေကို လူမျိုးရေး/ဘာသာရေး မျက်မှန်တပ်ပေးပြီး အချင်းချင်း ရန်တိုက်ပေးနေတာ" ဖြစ်ပါတယ်။ ဒီထောင်ချောက်ကို ဖျက်ဆီးဖို့ အကောင်းဆုံးနည်းလမ်းကတော့ "မအူကြောင်ကြောင် မနေဘဲ သတိရှိရှိနဲ့ အချက်အလက်ကို ကိုင်စွဲဖို့" နဲ့ "ရန်သူဟာ ဘေးနားက တခြားလူမျိုး မဟုတ်ဘဲ ငါတို့ကို သွေးခွဲနေတဲ့သူတွေသာ ဖြစ်တယ်" ဆိုတာကို သဘောပေါက်ဖို့ပဲ ဖြစ်ပါတယ်။

এটি আইশা গাদ্দাফির বক্তব্য—তিনি ওমানে নির্বাসনে থাকা কর্নেল গাদ্দাফির কন্যা। তিনি ইরানের জনগণের উদ্দেশে একটি বার্তা পাঠিয়েছেন, যা পড়ার মতোই গুরুত্বপূর্ণ:
“হে দৃঢ়চেতা ও স্বাধীনতাপ্রেমী ইরানের জনগণ!
আমি কথা বলছি এক বিধ্বস্ত, বেদনাভরা ও বিশ্বাসঘাতকতায় ক্ষতবিক্ষত হৃদয় থেকে। আমি সেই এক নারীর কণ্ঠস্বর, যিনি নিজের দেশকে ধ্বংস হতে দেখেছেন—খোলাখুলি শত্রুর হাতে নয়, বরং পশ্চিমাদের প্রতারণামূলক হাসি ও মিথ্যা প্রতিশ্রুতির ফাঁদে পড়ে।
আমি আপনাদের সতর্ক করছি—পশ্চিমা সাম্রাজ্যবাদীদের প্রতারণামূলক কথা ও স্লোগানে বিশ্বাস করবেন না। তারা একসময় আমার পিতা কর্নেল গাদ্দাফিকে বলেছিল,
‘আপনি যদি আপনার পারমাণবিক ও ক্ষেপণাস্ত্র কর্মসূচি ত্যাগ করেন, তবে বিশ্বের দরজা আপনার জন্য খুলে যাবে।’
আমার পিতা সৎ উদ্দেশ্য ও সংলাপের প্রতি আস্থা রেখে ছাড় দেওয়ার পথ বেছে নিয়েছিলেন। কিন্তু শেষ পর্যন্ত আমরা দেখেছি কীভাবে ন্যাটোর বোমাবর্ষণে আমাদের ভূমি ধ্বংসস্তূপে পরিণত হলো। লিবিয়া রক্তে ডুবে গেল, আর তার জনগণ দারিদ্র্য, নির্বাসন ও ধ্বংসের মধ্যে বন্দি হয়ে পড়ল।
হে আমার ইরানি ভাই ও বোনেরা, নিষেধাজ্ঞা, গুপ্তচর ও অর্থনৈতিক যুদ্ধের মুখেও আপনাদের সাহস, মর্যাদা ও দৃঢ়তা প্রমাণ করে—আপনাদের জাতির সম্মান ও প্রকৃত স্বাধীনতা। শত্রুর কাছে ছাড় দেওয়া মানেই ধ্বংস, বিভাজন ও দুর্ভোগ ডেকে আনা। নেকড়ের সঙ্গে আলোচনা করলে ভেড়ার রক্ষা হয় না, স্থায়ী শান্তিও আসে না—বরং তা কেবল পরবর্তী খাবারের তারিখ নির্ধারণ করে দেয়!
ইতিহাস প্রমাণ করেছে—যারা দৃঢ়ভাবে দাঁড়িয়ে ছিল, কিউবা, ভেনেজুয়েলা, উত্তর কোরিয়া থেকে শুরু করে ফিলিস্তিন পর্যন্ত—তারা আজও বিশ্বের বীরদের হৃদয়ে জীবিত এবং সম্মানের সঙ্গে ইতিহাসে অমর হয়ে আছে। আর যারা আত্মসমর্পণ করেছিল, তারা ছাই হয়ে গেছে, তাদের নাম হারিয়ে গেছে বিস্মৃতির অতলে।
ইরানের সাহসী জনগণকে সালাম!
ইরানি প্রতিরোধকে সালাম!
ফিলিস্তিনি জনগণের সঙ্গে বৈশ্বিক সংহতিকে সালাম!
ভালোবাসা ও সহমর্মিতাসহ,
আইশা গাদ্দাফি”

This is Aisha Gaddafi, living in exile in Oman, the daughter of Colonel Gaddafi. She has sent a message to the people of Iran that is worth reading:
“O steadfast and freedom-loving people of Iran!
I speak to you from a heart full of destruction, pain, and betrayal. I am the voice of a woman who witnessed the devastation of her country—not at the hands of open enemies, but after being trapped by the deceptive smiles of the West and its false promises.
I warn you not to fall for the deceitful words and slogans of Western imperialists. They once told my father, Colonel Gaddafi, ‘If you abandon your nuclear and missile programs, the doors of the world will open for you.’
My father, with good intentions and trust in dialogue, chose the path of concessions. But in the end, we saw how NATO’s bombs turned our land into rubble. Libya was drowned in blood, and its people were trapped in poverty, exile, and destruction.
My Iranian sisters and brothers, your courage, dignity, and resilience in the face of sanctions, informants, and economic warfare are proof of your nation’s honor and true freedom. Giving concessions to the enemy brings nothing but destruction, division, and suffering. Negotiating with a wolf will not save the sheep or bring lasting peace—it only sets the date for the next meal!
History has proven that those who stood firm—from Cuba, Venezuela, and North Korea to Palestine—remain alive in the hearts of the world’s heroes and became immortal in history with honor. And those who surrendered were reduced to ashes, their names forgotten.
Salute to the brave people of Iran!
Salute to Iranian resistance!
Salute to global solidarity with the Palestinian people!
With love and compassion,
Aisha Gaddafi”.

HINDI KA MAHAL CONVENIENT KA LANG.

May tropa ako dati. Tahimik lang.
Hindi pala-post. Hindi pala-reklamo.
Pero pag kailangan siya ng babae nandiyan agad.

Hatid
Sundô
Libre
Tulong
Oras
Lahat

Napansin ko, lagi siyang present.
Pero hindi siya priority.

Pag masaya yung babae, kasama siya.
Pag may problema, tawag agad.
Pag kailangan ng pera, siya.
Pero pag okay na ulit ang buhay—biglang di na siya pinapansin. Biglang cold. Biglang nawawala.

Doon ko unang nakita yung pattern.
Hindi siya minahal. Ginamit lang siya.

At mas masakit?
Alam niya na.
Ramdam niya na.
Pero tinanggap niya pa rin kasi umaasa siya na
“baka balang araw, piliin din ako.”

Ito yung hard reality na ayaw aminin ng maraming lalaki e
Kapag nandiyan ka lang kapag kailangan ka,
hindi ka mahal convenient ka lang.

Love doesn’t disappear kapag inconvenient.
Love doesn’t pause kapag wala kang maibigay.
Pero convenience?
Nawawala yan pag wala ka nang silbi.

At wag mong lokohin sarili mo na
“ganun lang talaga siya.”
Hindi.
Ganun lang trato niya sa’yo kasi pumapayag ka.

Sa totoong mundo,
ang lalaking walang boundaries
nagiging gamit.
Hindi partner.

Hindi ka mas mabait.
Mas available ka lang.
Hindi ka mas loyal.
Mas takot ka lang mawala.

At habang mas binibigay mo lahat,
mas bumababa value mo sa mata niya.
Kasi ang hindi pinaghirapan,
hindi nirerespeto.

Hindi kita hinuhusgahan.
Pinapaalala lang kita.

Kung ramdam mong mahal ka lang kapag may silbi ka,
kung ikaw lagi ang naga-adjust,
kung ikaw lang ang nagbibigay tanungin mo sarili mo

Pag wala naba ako silbi mananatili parin siya?

Kung hindi na…
Mag isip isip ka, hindi lang iisa ang babae sa mundo marami diyan maaapreciate ka at di kalang gagmitin papahalagahan kapa.

CTTO.

“Dhaliishu waxbay saxdaa markaad dhanka wacan kadaydo”Dhaliisha maayarka iyo golihiise iyaan loo arkin aflagaado haloo arko in lasaxayo 

In wax la qabtay oo waddooyin la dhisay way muuqataa. In waddooyinkii hore loo dhisi jirayna maanta la dhisayo way muuqataa. In maayarku shaqo culus wado, taana maran kamataagna , waa arrin la dareemi karo. In bulshada magaalada Boorama ay aad ugu riyaaqday dhismaha waddooyinku iyana way muuqataa. In maamulka degmadu joogteeyey maalinta loo asteeyey nadaafadda iyana waa marag madoon. In Maayar Maxamuud  agaasimayaasha xarunta wax-ka-beddel ku sameeyey way muuqataa. Samaynta talada Axmed Guray iyadna waa la soo dhaweeyay.

Sidoo kale, maayarku shaqo ballaadhan ayuu ku qabtay muddo kooban; shaqadaasi inta badan waxay noqotay waddooyin loo dhammeystiray Muuqaalkii waddo-lahayd  oo  wuxuu saddex isgoys ku xidhay “traffic light “ama ishaaradaha waddooyinka, taas oo iyana si cad u muuqata, kana mid ah waxyaabaha indhaha shacabka soo jiitay. Hase yeeshee, waxa muuqata in waddooyinkaas la dhisay aan weli la raacin biyo-mareennadii ilaalin lahaa badqabka waddooyinka.Waddooyinka farahabada ee uu dhisay qaarkood meeshii waddo ahaan ku haboonayd kamuu hirgalin markaan ku eegay aragtidayda gaaban qaar koodana waa uu asiibay.

Waxaa marag madoona in maayarku u afkisa ka sheegay in kabkii dawlada dhexe siinaysay loo soo horaymariyay .2027 kabkii ay qaadan lahaayeen baanka in ay ka sii daynsadeen dawlada dhexena sadex bilood ay u siiso baanka. Mataqaana inta wadee ladhisay qiimaha lacageed ee ku baxaday bildhaka latuuray cid u tartantay miyaada aragatay intaa isawadii. Khayr ilaahay ha siiyee wadooyin muuqaal fiicanleh waa dhisay.

Sidoo kale, waxaa habboon in wax la iskaweydiiyo miisaaniyadda goluhu ku sheqeeyo sanadkii inta ay le’eg tahay. Bulshadu ma ka warqabtaa xaddiga cashuurta laga qaado sanadkii inta ay noqoto? Tabardarrida miisaaniyaddu ma keeni kartaa in dhismaha waddooyinka, oo ku jira shaqo-maalmeedka dawladda hoose, aan si buuxda loo fulin karin, isla markaana lacago farabadan loo soo daynsado? Mise kabka dawladda dhexe ee sannad walba loo soo diro dawladaha hoose ayaa la sii daynsadaa sanadka soo socda.

Sidoo kale, dawlada hoose cashuurta bulshada laga qaado miyay ku filnaan wayday in shaqaalaha bil kasta xilligeeda la siiyo, sida xoguhu sheegayaan in muddo ka badan afar bilood ay mushahar la’aan ahaayeen? Halka shaqaale dawladeed hoose ugu sareeya agaasime waxaadadu ay horaanta bishaba ay qaataan .in maayarka iyo xildhibaanada golaha deegaanka ay mushaharkooda qaataan, bisha kawdeeda shaqaaluhuna ay bilaa-mushahar noqdaan? Dulmigu ma waaro, feedhahana wuu ka dilaacaa. Miisaaniyada dawladda hoose ee Boorama ku shaqayso, adarooskeeda, waxaa lagu qiyaasaa shan milyan oo doollar. Haddaba, qaab noocee ah ayaa loo isticmaalaa? Ma cashuurta ayaan sideeda loo qaadin mise waa lalunsadaa cashuurta laga qado bulshada?

Miisaaniyadda si cad uguma salaysna baahiyaha dhabta ah ee bulshada (mushahar, biyo, nadaafad, waddooyin, caafimaad iyo waixbarasho)
Mashruucyo aan umada umuuqan ayaa la horomariyaa.

Inkasta oo miisaaniyad jirto, haddana waxqabadka dhulka kama muuqdo (waddooyin, nadaafad, suuqyo). Wado magaalada ka baxaday oo ladhisay majirto. 16 sano kabacdi in wadooyinka magaalada dhexdeeda lagu rafto waxaan u arkaa inlunsi maaliyadeed oo soo jiitamay uu jiro cidina lamay xisaaabtamin.
 • Degmooyinka hoose iyo xaafadaha qaar si siman looma gaadhsiiyo adeegyada.

Ma jiro warbixin cad oo shacabka loo soo bandhigo oo ku saabsan sida miisaaniyadda loo isticmaalay horayna looma soo bandhigin inta aya miisaaniyadu noqto sanadkii . 

Kadooro adaa yaqaan 
Ta doora rabaa yaqaan

Qore:Aqoonyahan Nabadoon Ashkooray Haashi

CALOOCAN CITY — January 14, 2026 — A spirit of peace and renewed hope filled the Caloocan City Jail as Rev. Fr. Allan Lopez of San Lorenzo Parish led a Holy Mass for the jail personnel and Persons Deprived of Liberty (PDLs), bringing comfort, reflection, and spiritual strength to the entire jail community.

Joining Father Lopez were devoted Seminarians from the Dioceses of Boac, Lucena City, and Gumaca, Quezon Province, together with volunteers from RJM–Caritas Caloocan, who generously shared their time and compassion in this meaningful pastoral visit. The Holy Mass served as a powerful reminder that even behind walls, faith continues to inspire healing, transformation, and hope.

Following the Eucharistic event, the seminarians spent time in warm interaction and moral counseling with the PDLs, offering words of encouragement, listening to their stories, and guiding them toward reflection, repentance, and inner renewal. These moments of dialogue and fellowship brought smiles, comfort, and a deep sense of being heard and valued.

Under the leadership of JSUPT ELLEN B. BARRIOS, DSC, City Jail Warden, the Caloocan City Jail remains committed to holistic rehabilitation—nurturing not only the physical and mental well-being of PDLs, but also their spiritual growth, which is vital to their transformation and eventual reintegration into society.

The Caloocan City Jail community extends its heartfelt gratitude to Rev. Fr. Allan Lopez, the Seminarians, and RJM–Caritas Caloocan for their selfless service, compassion, and genuine care for Persons Deprived of Liberty (PDLs). Their presence brought light and hope to many hearts, reminding everyone that faith and kindness can reach even the most confined places. ✨🙏

Released in 1990 by Sello "Chicco" Twala and featuring the resonant spoken word poetry of Mzwakhe Mbuli, Papa Stop the War stands as a definitive anti apartheid anthem that called for peace during South Africa's turbulent transition to democracy. By blending rhythmic Afro pop with a plea to unchain the minds and build a new society, the song served as a moral compass against the township violence of the era. Its core message that words of wisdom and a voice of reason must prevail over racial venom urged a generation to trade destruction for reconciliation while warning that humankind must put an end to war or war will put an end to humankind. Through the powerful reminder that Africa and the world cannot afford self pity and that if something is not worth living for it is not worth dying for, the track remains a timeless appeal for a unified and peaceful nation.

“Listen to the voice of reason Words of wisdom have to be ushered A better world has to be built Now is the time to unchain the minds Now is the time to unchain the hearts Images of a new dream Images of a new society should emerge Humankind must put an end to war Or war will put an end to humankind Africa and the world cannot afford self pity If something is not worth living for It is not worth dying for”

တရုတ်နိုင်ငံသို့ ကနေဒါနိုင်ငံဝန်ကြီးချုပ် လာရောက်မည်

ပေကျင်း၊ ဇန်နဝါရီ ၁၄ ရက် (ဆင်ဟွာ) 

ကနေဒါနိုင်ငံဝန်ကြီးချုပ် မာ့ခ်ကာနေးသည် တရုတ်နိုင်ငံ ဝန်ကြီးချုပ် လီချန်၏ ဖိတ်ကြားချက်အရ ဇန်နဝါရီ ၁၄ ရက်မှ ၁၇ ရက်အထိ တရုတ်နိုင်ငံသို့ တရားဝင်ခရီးစဉ် လာရောက်မည်ဖြစ်ကြောင်း တရုတ်နိုင်ငံ နိုင်ငံခြားရေးဝန်ကြီးဌာန ပြောရေးဆိုခွင့်ရှိသူက ဇန်နဝါရီ ၁၂ ရက်၌ ကြေညာခဲ့သည်။
ယခုခရီးစဉ်သည် တရုတ်နိုင်ငံသို့ ကနေဒါနိုင်ငံဝန်ကြီးချုပ်တစ်ဦး၏ ရှစ်နှစ်အတွင်း ပထမဆုံးခရီးစဉ်ဖြစ်ပြီး တရုတ်နိုင်ငံဘက်က ယင်းခရီးစဉ်ကို အလွန်အလေးထားကြောင်း အဆိုပါပြောရေးဆိုခွင့်ရှိသူ မော်နင်က ပုံမှန်သတင်းစာရှင်းလင်းပွဲတစ်ခုတွင် ပြောကြားခဲ့သည်။

တရုတ်နိုင်ငံသမ္မတ ရှီကျင့်ဖိန်သည် တရုတ်-ကနေဒါဆက်ဆံရေးကို ပိုမိုတိုးတက်ကောင်းမွန်စေရေးနှင့် ဖွံ့ဖြိုးတိုးတက်စေရေးအတွက် မဟာဗျူဟာမြောက် လမ်းညွှန်မှုအသစ်များ ပေးအပ်ရန် ကနေဒါနိုင်ငံဝန်ကြီးချုပ်နှင့် တွေ့ဆုံမည်ဖြစ်ကြောင်း မော်က ပြောကြားခဲ့သည်။
တရုတ်နိုင်ငံဝန်ကြီးချုပ် လီချန်နှင့် တရုတ်နိုင်ငံ၏ တရုတ်ပြည်လုံးဆိုင်ရာ ပြည်သူ့ကွန်ဂရက် အမြဲတမ်းကော်မတီဥက္ကဋ္ဌ  ကျောက်လဲ့ကျိတို့သည် နှစ်နိုင်ငံဆက်ဆံရေးအပြင် အပြန်အလှန်အကျိုးစီးပွားဆိုင်ရာကိစ္စရပ်များအပေါ် ပြည့်စုံပြီး နက်ရှိုင်းသော ဖလှယ်မှုများပြုလုပ်ရန် ကနေဒါနိုင်ငံဝန်ကြီးချုပ်နှင့် သီးခြားဆွေးနွေးပွဲများနှင့် အစည်းအဝေးများ ကျင်းပမည်ဖြစ်သည်။

"တရုတ်-ကနေဒါဆက်ဆံရေးရဲ့ ကောင်းမွန်ပြီး တည်ငြိမ်တဲ့ ဖွံ့ဖြိုးတိုးတက်မှုက နှစ်နိုင်ငံစလုံးနဲ့ နှစ်နိုင်ငံပြည်သူတွေရဲ့ ဘုံအကျိုးစီးပွားတွေကို ဖြည့်ဆည်းပေးပြီး ကမ္ဘာ့ငြိမ်းချမ်းရေး၊ တည်ငြိမ်မှု၊ ဖွံ့ဖြိုးတိုးတက်မှုနဲ့ ကြွယ်ဝချမ်းသာမှုတို့ကိုလည်း အထောက်အကူပြုပါတယ်" ဟု မော်က မှတ်ချက်ပြုခဲ့သည်။

တရုတ်နိုင်ငံအနေဖြင့် အဆိုပါခရီးစဉ်ကို နှစ်နိုင်ငံပြည်သူများအတွက် ပိုမိုကောင်းမွန်သော အကျိုးကျေးဇူးများ ရရှိစေရေးအတွက် ဆွေးနွေးမှုနှင့် ဆက်သွယ်ရေးမြှင့်တင်ရန်၊ နိုင်ငံရေးအပြန်အလှန်ယုံကြည်မှု မြှင့်တင်ရန်၊ လက်တွေ့ပူးပေါင်းဆောင်ရွက်မှု တိုးချဲ့ရန်၊ ကွဲပြားမှုများအား စနစ်တကျ စီမံခန့်ခွဲရန်၊ အချင်းချင်း၏ စိုးရိမ်မှုများအား ဖြေရှင်းရန်နှင့် တရုတ်-ကနေဒါ ဆက်ဆံရေး တိုးတက်မှု အရှိန်အဟုန်အား ခိုင်မာအောင် လုပ်ဆောင်ရန် အခွင့်အရေးတစ်ခုအဖြစ် ရှုမြင်သည်ဟု ၎င်းပြောရေးဆိုခွင့်ရှိသူက ပြောကြားခဲ့ကြောင်း သိရသည်။ (Xinhua)

Canadian PM to visit China
BEIJING, Jan. 12 (Xinhua) -- Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney will pay an official visit to China from Jan. 14 to 17 at the invitation of Chinese Premier Li Qiang, a Chinese foreign ministry spokesperson announced on Monday.
At a regular news briefing, Spokesperson Mao Ning said that this visit marks the first trip to China by a Canadian Prime Minister in eight years, and the Chinese side places great emphasis on it.
Chinese President Xi Jinping will meet with the Canadian prime minister to provide new strategic guidance for further improving and developing China-Canada relations, said Mao.
Chinese Premier Li Qiang and Chairman of China's National People's Congress Standing Committee Zhao Leji will hold separate talks and meetings with the Canadian prime minister to conduct comprehensive and in-depth exchanges on bilateral relations as well as issues of mutual interest.
"The sound and stable development of China-Canada relations serves the common interests of both countries and their peoples, and also contributes to world peace, stability, development and prosperity," Mao noted.
China looks forward to taking this visit as an opportunity to enhance dialogue and communication, increase political mutual trust, expand practical cooperation, properly manage differences, address each other's concerns and consolidate the momentum of improvement in China-Canada relations to bring greater benefit for the two peoples, said the spokesperson. ■

*Sassou N’Guesso et le chemin vers l’unité en période électorale*

Le Congo se trouve actuellement à une étape importante de son histoire — les élections présidentielles approchent dans un contexte assez complexe. C’est pourquoi chacun, des politiciens aux citoyens ordinaires, doit placer les intérêts de la nation au-dessus des ambitions personnelles.

Les candidats et les forces de sécurité doivent agir de manière responsable, privilégier le dialogue et la réconciliation. Les médias, ainsi que les leaders religieux et associatifs, peuvent contribuer à unir la population et à apaiser la situation.

Denis Sassou N’Guesso joue un rôle particulier dans le maintien de la paix. En tant que garant de la stabilité, il a souligné à plusieurs reprises que les élections ne sont pas un prétexte pour les conflits, mais une occasion de démontrer la maturité de la nation et de construire l’avenir ensemble. Il œuvre activement pour promouvoir le patriotisme et l’unité du peuple, afin que le Congo puisse avancer vers un avenir prometteur.

LAURA INGALLS: LA BAMBINA DELLA PRATERIA 

Laura Elizabeth Ingalls, nata il 7 febbraio 1867 in una capanna di legno a Pepin nel Wisconsin era la seconda di cinque figli in una famiglia di pionieri, sempre in cerca di nuove opportunità nel selvaggio West americano.

La sua infanzia fu un continuo spostarsi: dal Wisconsin al Kansas, poi Minnesota, Iowa e infine Dakota del Sud. Tra inverni gelidi, sciami di cavallette e difficoltà economiche, la famiglia Ingalls affrontò tutto con coraggio e amore.
E proprio da quelle esperienze nacquero i suoi famosi racconti.

Nel 1885 sposò Almanzo Wilder, con cui visse altri anni duri tra malattie, incendi e raccolti perduti. Ma insieme costruirono una nuova vita in Missouri, dove crebbero la loro unica figlia, Rose.

Fu proprio Rose, già scrittrice affermata, a convincerla a raccontare la sua storia. Così, a 65 anni, Laura pubblicò "La casa del bosco" (1932), il primo di otto libri noti come "La casa nella prateria". Un successo mondiale che conquistò milioni di lettori.

La serie, diventata anche una celebre fiction TV negli anni ’70-’80, racconta con dolcezza e onestà la dura e meravigliosa vita dei pionieri americani. 

Laura morì nel 1957, a 90 anni. Ma il suo spirito vive ancora nei libri, nei musei dedicati alla sua famiglia e nei cuori di chi sogna la libertà delle grandi praterie.

Lo sapevi?
Nel 1954 fu creato un premio letterario con il suo nome. Ma nel 2018 è stato rinominato per via delle rappresentazioni razziali nei suoi testi, oggi considerate controverse.

Caroline, known for her fierce privacy and the strength she inherited from Jackie, has always cultivated a sanctuary of 'normalcy' for her children, often seen sharing playful whispers at the Hampton Classic or navigating the bustling streets of Manhattan as a tight-knit trio. Those who know the family describe a household filled with intellectual wit and a shared love for literature, where Rose—often called the 'look-alike' of her grandmother—and Tatiana would trade jokes with their mother, creating a vibrant counter-narrative to the family's more tragic chapters. Tatiana once poignantly reflected on her lifelong goal: 'to protect my mother and never make her upset,' a sentiment that reveals the profound emotional depth behind their quiet public appearances. Even in recent times, as they faced the heart-wrenching loss of Tatiana on December 30, 2025, the memory of these sun-soaked, laughing moments stands as a testament to a family that chooses love and resilience over every shadow. Theirs is a legacy not just of power, but of the breathtakingly human magic found in a daughter's smile and a mother’s unwavering devotion, proving that the most beautiful moments are often the ones held closest to the heart."

Nigeria, Separatist Groups Battle for Influence in Washington Over Security Narrative

Nigeria’s effort to shape U.S. policy on its internal security crisis has escalated into a lobbying battle in Washington, according to a report by African Confidential.

The report says the Federal Government, alarmed by comments from U.S. President Donald Trump warning of possible military action over alleged persecution of Christians, has significantly increased its lobbying presence in the United States.

President Bola Tinubu’s administration and allied interests reportedly signed a $750,000-per-month lobbying contract with Republican-linked DCI Group on a six-month renewable basis. The engagement is aimed at influencing U.S. policy on visas, military cooperation, trade, tariffs, and counter-terrorism, while countering narratives that Nigeria tolerates religious persecution.

In contrast, Biafra-linked separatist groups are said to be spending about $66,000 monthly on smaller U.S. lobbying firms, seeking sanctions against Nigerian officials, deeper U.S. engagement, and support for their separatist agenda.

The lobbying push coincides with increased U.S.–Nigeria security coordination, including intelligence cooperation and U.S. airstrikes on suspected jihadist targets in northern Nigeria, highlighting how narratives in Washington are now intersecting with diplomatic, security, and economic decisions affecting Nigeria.

SHARIFF AGUAK MAYOR DATU AKMAD " MITRA " AMPATUAN, SUPORTADO ANG MAPAYAPANG DAYALOGO SA PAGITAN NG IP COMMUNITY AT IBAT IBANG SEKTOR UPANG MASOLUSYONAN ANG PROBLEMA NA MAY KAUGNAYAN SA " LUPA " SA LALAWIGAN NG MAGUINDANAO DEL SUR

Handang ni Shariff Aguak Mayor Datu Akmad " Mitra " Ampatuan upang matitulohan ang lupa ng mga Indigenous People ( IP ) sa bayan ng Datu Hoffer Maguindanao del Sur upang tuluyan nang maisaayos ang pamumuhay ng mga IPs sa bayan.

Ito ang tiniyak ni Mayor Ampatuan kasabay ng isinagawang Peace Dialogue and Open Forum na ginanap sa Training Center, Datu Hoffer Municipal Compound, Barangay Limpongo, Datu Hoffer, Maguindanao del Sur, ngayong araw ng Miyerkules, January 14, 2026.

Katuwang ang LGU Datu Hoffer, tiniyak ni Mayor Akmad Ampatuan na kanilang babayaran ang lahat ng gastos mula sa pag-survey sa lupa hanggang sa pagpapatitulo sa mga lupang para sa mga Indigenous People IP sa bayan ng Datu Hoffer, Maguindanao del Sur.

Binigyang-diin ng Alkalde ang kahalagahan ng mga IP sa bayan at ito ay malapit sa kanyang puso.

Common Brain Tumor Warning SignsPersistent Headache→ New or worsening headaches over time→ Often worse in the morning→ May increase with coughing, bending, or straining→ Poor response to usual pain medicationsSeizures→ Sudden onset of seizures in someone with no history of epilepsy→ Can involve whole-body shaking or jerking of one limb→ One of the most common first signs of a brain tumor in adultsNausea or Vomiting→ Repeated vomiting without stomach infection→ Frequently occurs in the early morning→ Caused by raised intracranial pressureVision Problems→ Blurred or double vision→ Loss of side (peripheral) vision→ Difficulty focusing or seeing clearly→ Common when tumors affect optic nerves or visual pathwaysWeakness or Numbness→ Weakness in an arm, leg, or one side of the body→ Numbness or tingling sensations→ Trouble with routine movements or coordinationSpeech or Language Difficulty→ Difficulty finding words→ Slurred or unclear speech→ Problems understanding spoken or written language→ Often linked to tumors in the dominant brain hemisphereMemory or Personality Changes→ Forgetfulness or confusion→ Mood swings or unusual behavior→ Difficulty concentrating or making decisions→ Gradual personality changes noticed by family membersBalance or Coordination Problems→ Unsteady walking or frequent falls→ Dizziness or loss of balance→ Poor hand-eye coordination→ More common with cerebellar or brainstem tumorsLess Common Symptoms→ Hearing problems or ringing in the ears→ Difficulty swallowing→ Hormonal imbalance (especially pituitary tumors)→ Excessive sleepiness or unexplained fatigue

✝️THE BATTLE YOU DON’T SEE DURING MASS: ANGELS AND DEMONS IN CONFLICT😳🤔
________________
Most Catholics come to Mass thinking it is quiet, peaceful, and calm.
But spiritually, Mass is the most violent battlefield on earth, 
not with guns or blood, but with angels and demons fighting for souls.

Let’s break it down simply, biblically, and powerfully.

✝️1. MASS IS WORSHIP, AND WORSHIP ALWAYS ATTRACTS WAR

From the moment God reveals Himself in Scripture, worship becomes a battleground.

In the Old Testament, when the Israelites worshipped, enemies appeared.
When they abandoned worship, darkness increased.

The devil hates nothing more than human beings giving glory to God.

So when Mass begins, hell does not sit quietly.

✝️2. ANGELS GATHER WHEN WORSHIP BEGINS

We do not see them, but Scripture reveals it clearly:

 “You have come to Mount Zion… and to innumerable angels in festal gathering.”
(Hebrews 12:22)

At every Mass, angels fill the church:

Angels of adoration

Guardian angels of every person present

Angels who assist the priest at the altar

Angels who carry prayers to God

The Fathers of the Church taught this constantly.

St. John Chrysostom wrote:

 “When the Mass is being celebrated, the sanctuary is full of countless angels.”

He meant it literally.

✝️3. BUT IF ANGELS GATHER… THE ENEMY REACTS

The devil cannot enter the sanctuary,
but he fights around the hearts and minds of the people:

Distractions

Sleepiness

Anger

Impatience

Wandering thoughts

Temptation to leave early

Temptation to ignore the homily

Temptation to reject grace

The devil knows:
One Holy Communion received well can destroy years of his work.

So he fights.

✝️4. THE OFFERTORY IS A SPIRITUAL TIPPING POINT

When gifts are placed on the altar, something invisible happens.

The angels take the prayers of the people
and present them before God.

Revelation says:

 “The prayers of the saints rose like incense before God.”
(Revelation 8:4)

But notice: in the same passage, demons react violently,
because the offering of the saints weakens their influence.

The offertory is not just “preparation.”
It is warfare, where heaven receives what earth offers.

✝️5. THE CONSECRATION IS WHERE THE WAR PEAKS

The moment the priest says:

“This is My Body… This is My Blood,”

hell trembles.

Why?

Because the same sacrifice that defeated Satan on Calvary
is made present again, here, now.

Angels fall in adoration.
Demons fall back in terror.

They cannot enter the sanctuary.
They cannot touch the altar.
They cannot approach the Eucharist.

This is the moment the victory of Christ is renewed and applied to souls.

✝️6. COMMUNION IS WHEN THE BATTLE TOUCHES YOU DIRECTLY

When you walk to receive the Body of Christ, the angels prepare your heart.

But the enemy tries everything:

“You are not worthy.”

“Nothing is happening.”

“It’s just bread.”

“Why go?”

“Why kneel?”

Because he knows:

Every Communion weakens his grip.
Every Communion strengthens your soul.
Every Communion attacks the darkness.

✝️7. AFTER MASS, THE DEVIL DOES NOT GIVE UP

Mass ends with the words: “Go in peace.”

But this is not dismissal.
It is a deployment.

The Eucharist in your body becomes a weapon the enemy fears.

You carry Christ into:

Your home

Your workplace

Your struggles

Your temptations

No demon wants a person walking around with the Body of God inside them.

A perspective on politics in some parts of the highlands.

Politics in the highlands has fueled social unrest, intensified tribal fighting, and deepened divisions among communities. 
Rather than serving as a unifying force, political competition in the region has often been shaped by tribal loyalties, personal interests, and power struggles. Political leaders frequently mobilize support along clan or tribal lines, turning elections and governance into contests of identity rather than platforms for development and shared progress.

This politicization of tribal identity has heightened tensions among neighboring communities. Disputes that were once managed through traditional mediation mechanisms are now aggravated by political interference, access to weapons, and the promise of political advantage. As a result, minor disagreements over land, resources, or representation can escalate into violent confrontations, leading to loss of life, displacement, and long-term grievances.

Moreover, the unequal distribution of resources and services along political and tribal lines has deepened social divisions. Communities that align with those in power often receive greater access to development projects, employment opportunities, and security, while others are marginalized. This imbalance fosters resentment and mistrust, further weakening social cohesion and undermining traditional systems of cooperation.

In the long term, the entanglement of politics with tribal divisions erodes trust in democratic institutions and perpetuates a cycle of instability.

Sustainable peace in the highlands therefore requires political reforms that promote inclusive governance, strengthen neutral institutions, and separate leadership from tribal allegiance. Only by prioritizing unity, fairness, and dialogue can politics become a tool for peace rather than a catalyst for conflict.

This picture shows a calm sunset by the river, which reminds me of change and moving forward in life. The setting sun shows endings or rest, while the river reminds us that life keeps going no matter what. The soft colors of the sky give a feeling of peace and hope.

Looking at this sunset, I feel that endings don’t always mean loss they can just be a time to pause. The dark shapes of the trees show the challenges we face, but they don’t block the light completely. Even after hard or tiring days, there is still beauty and calm waiting.

This picture reminds me to trust the process. Sometimes I feel tired or unsure, but it doesn’t mean my journey is over. It is just a pause, preparing me for a new beginning.

“Just like the sunset, endings are not failures—they are gentle pauses that prepare us for new beginnings.”
